21: pret. oAAx, freq. of ay: nitla — to do something several times, often, frequently.
22: or AHAYOUIA, pret. OAAYOUI, etc., freq. of avoura: nitla — to warm, to reheat something with one's breath.
23: pret. Oaari, freq. of auTIA: nino (for nino) — to wash oneself, to bathe. Nite — to wash, to bathe someone.
24: pret. OAAMAN, freq. of AMaNa: nino — to worry, to torment oneself greatly. Nite — to cause pain to someone, to torment them.
25: pret. OAAMAPOUR, freq. of amapoa: n (for ni) — to read much, often.
28: from ameyazLo. Flowing, that which flows out.
29: pret. o4an, freq. of ami: n (for ni) — to hunt in various places, to hunt often.
30: n. v. Hunter in various places. R. aami.
31: pret. OAANTINEN: n (for ni) — to walk while hunting. RR. aami, nenu.
32: pret. OAAMOXITO, freq. of AMOXITOA: n (for ni) — to read much, often.
33: pret. OAAMOxPOUH, freq. of AMOxPOA: n (for ni) — to read often, much.
34: Long lock of hair that the Indians left on young girls on only one side of the head. See Arzorzocozur.
35: pret. oaaw, freq. of ana: nin (for nino) — to amuse oneself, to stroll, to recreate oneself, to take pleasure, to stretch out; otit-aanque (for otito-aanque) we seized each other's hands, we took each other by the hands, to dance or do something else; m-aana (for mo-aana), he stretches out, stretches himself, it is flexible, elastic (cosa correosa como ul). Nitla or nic — to take a thing; itlan or itzalan nitla-aana, to choose a thing from among others, to extract it.
36: pret. OAAPOPOTIC, freq. of apopori: n (for ni) — to swim underwater.
37: pret. OAAQUETZ, freq. of AQUETZA: n (for ni) — to raise and lower one's head frequently like a madman.
38: pret. o44c, freq. of aquI: n (for ni) — to rejoice, to be very content.
39: pret. oAAG: n (for ni) — to do something imperfectly, to make mistakes, to commit errors.
164: or ACALQUIXOUAYAN, n. v. Seaport; lit. place through which ships enter and exit. RR. acalli, quixoayan.
165: Pret. OACGALQUIXTI to put a ship aground. RR. acalli, quxtia.
166: Harbor, embarkation point, anchorage for ships. RR. acalli, teca.
167: Pret. OACALTEN to equip a ship. RR. acalli, tema.
168: dim. of acazur. Small ship, sloop, canoe, small boat.
169: Pret. ACALTETEN: (for ni) or nitla — to ballast a ship. RR. acaltetl, tema.
170: Venomous snake; a kind of lizard (Clav.).
171: Often accompanied by tlatilinca-: n (for ni) — : mila — to load a teconi. Ship's ballast. RR. acalli, tetl.
173: With or on a vessel: acaltica n-aci, to navigate to the end, to spend one's life at sea; acallica nemiliztli or panoliztli, navigation; lit. life or voyage at sea; acaltica nemini, navigator, sailor; acaltica panoni, passenger. RR. acalli, ca.
174: dim. of acazu. Small ship, boat, sloop, any object having the shape of a boat, as in copalacaltontli, incense boat.
175: Pret. OACALUELTECAC: — to steer the ship with the rudder. RR. acalli, n (for ni) uelteca.
176: or ACALUELTECANI, n. v. One who steers the ship with the rudder, pilot who holds the rudder. R. acaluelteca.
177: n. of instrument. Rudder: lit. that which serves to steer the ship. R. acaluelteca.
179: (for Acamapichtzin), the elder, king of Colhuacan (Chim.); — the younger, first king of Mexico (Chim., Clav.). R. acamapilli.
181: Nowhere, in no place: acampa niaz, I will go nowhere. RR. acan, pa.
122: Pret. OACALAQUI: to plunge, to put something in water. RR. atl, calaqua.
124: Pilot who stands at the stern of the ship. RR. acalli, cuexcochtia (?).
125: Stern, rear of the vessel. RR. acalli, cuexcochtli.
126: Pret. OACALHUI: nino (for nino) — to sail for pleasure; nitla — to transport something by boat; nitla — to submerge by boat. R. acalli.
127: Prow, front, bow of the ship. RR. acalli, yacatl.
128: Bilge; lit. stench, filth of the ship. RR. acalli, iyayaliztli.
129: n. of instrument. Rudder, helm; lit. that which serves to steer the ship. RR. acalli, matl.
130: Pret. OACALLAZ: ni (for ni) or nitla — to launch a ship. RR. acalli, tlaça.
131: Pilot; lit. he who steers the ship, or who watches over it; acallachiani in teachcauh, first pilot, chief pilot. RR. acalli, tlachiani or tlachixqui.
134: Bilge; lit. filth of the ship. RR. acalli, tlayayaliztli.
135: He who puts a ship aground. R. acallaluaccaquixtia.
137: ni (for ni) — to put a ship aground. RR. acalli, tlaluacqui, quixtia.
138: n. v. lit. he who takes care of the ship. See Acazcuitlauti. Sailor, seaman; acalcuitlauti. RR. acalli, cuitlauti.
139: prov., Cap. Jeancanac (Clav.); town, now Acala, to the S.W. of Mexico (O. yB.).
242: ACHCHICA or ACHICHICAN, adv. Often, frequently, continually, unceasingly: achica ni-tlaqua, to eat often. Sometimes it means quite far: achica onantica, it is quite far, there is a long journey from here to that place. Preceded by ça, çan or huel, this adv. marks the superlative: ça achica in ti-tlaqua, you eat incessantly, very often, extraordinarily; achichican tlanauatia, imperious, one who commands and orders a great deal; oc cuel achica, soon, in a moment.
243: HUITZNAHUATL (Don Juan Garcia), nineteenth child of King Axayucatl (Chim.).
244: Weak, without strength, speaking of the limbs of the body. RR. a priv. chicauac.
245: Weakness, debility. RR. a priv. chcaualiztli.
246: Spring, fountain. With the postposition pan: achichiacpan, at the fountain or the fountain itself. RR. atl, chichia (?).
248: from the Campine region, which supplied cacao to the kingdom of Acolhuacan (Clav.).
249: Wooden hook for drawing water. RR. atl, chicolli.
251: pret. OACHIYOTEUI to mix ochre with other colors. R. achiyotetl.
252: pret. OACHIYOTL: nitla — to paint something with ochre. R. achiyotl.
253: (Clav.). Annatto, a fruit used in dyeing (achiote).
256: Edible herb that grows in water. RR. atl, chilli, quilül.
257: (?) adv. Well, or achin canin, where it is necessary, there where one must, by the way one must go. RR. ach, in.
261: pret. OACHIPANQUETZ: nin (for nino) — to make oneself greater, to rise up. RR. achi, ipan, quetza.: nitla — to mix: achin cayepa, achin campa
322: 5. A kind of herb that grows near springs and marshes.
323: Parasitic plants that grow on trees.
324: A place on the southern shore of the lake of Mexico (Clav.), where the Aztecs stayed for seventeen years (Bét.).
327: Delicacy of temperament. RR. priv. a. cocohztlapaliuilizth.
329: Plant resembling fennel, or instrument for extracting the sap of the agave. RR. al. Gentleness, mildness. cocotl.
330: Light, not heavy, that rises easily. R. acocui.
331: pret. oacoc or oacocu (Olm.): nino — to rise, to get up, to rear up, to fly up, nin (to take flight, etc.); m-acocui, it grows, increases, rises, speaking of a watercourse. Nita or nic — to lift a thing; nic-acocui in uentli ixpantzinco in Dios, to make an offering to God by raising it into the air.
333: One of the Aztec chiefs who founded Tenochtitlan (Clav.).
335: Elevation: teyollo acoyaliztli, contemplation. R. acoyauh.
336: 5. v. One who rises: iyollo acoyan, contemplative; lit. one who raises his heart, his spirit. R. acoyauh.
337: pret. OACOYA: (for ni) — to look upward, to raise one's eyes, to contemplate; acoyauh in noyollo, my heart goes upward, I contemplate, I consider things from above. RR. aco, yauh.
338: Canal, sewer, drain. RR. atl, coyoctli.
339: A bird with red plumage, called comendador by the Spanish (Clav.). RR. acolh, chichi.
340: Shoulder blade. In comp. tacolchimal (for to-acolchimal), our shoulder blade, the shoulder blade in general. RR. acolli, chimalh.
341: A place where the Chichimecs stayed for fifty-two years (1262–1314) (Clav.).
342: pret. OACOLEUH: nitla — to raise the arm, to threaten. RR. acolh, eua.
41: pret. o4AQuI, freq. of AquIA: mitla or mic — to put a thing several times, to drive in, to hide; chilli nic-aaquia, to transplant chili. Fig. na, vexi, itlan c-aaquia, he makes no account of his parents or his own people; lit. he hides his hands, his feet, his teeth (el que no haze caso de sus deudos por estar el rico).
43: pret. OAATEN, freq. of ATEMI — to be pale, haggard, swollen.
45: pret. OaaTiLI, freq. of ATILIA: mn (for nino) — to stretch oneself, to extend; m-aatilia (for mo-aatila), he stretches, extends himself; it is flexible, elastic.
46: one of the chiefs of the army of Quinatzin (Dé)
50: pret. OAAUIx, etc. freq. of aura: n (for m) — to rejoice, to take pleasure, to glory, to boast; teca or tepan n-aauia, to rejoice at another's misfortune, with regard to someone. Also written aahuia (Par.). Rev. aauiltia (Olm.).
51: Or AHAUIALIA, pret. OAAUJALI, etc.: nin (for nino) — to lick oneself, to savor. R. aauia.
54: With joy, pleasure, gladness; teca ahawializhica, laughing at someone, with mockery, derision. RR. aauializth, ca.
55: With honeyed, treacherous advice (Olm.). RR. aauializ- tlamachializth, ca.
56: Honeyed, treacherous advice. RR. aawializtli, tlamachializth.
57: Or AHAUIALIZTLI, n. Joy, gladness, glory, vanity; teca or tepan ahauializth, mockery. With the postposition pan: aauializpan, in joy, with gladness. R. aauia.
58: adj. Gay, joyful, content, playful, fond of joy and pleasures, courtesan. R. aauia.
59: pret. OAAUIGTLAZ, freq. of AUrCYLAGA: nite — to repel, to reject someone with violence.
60: or AHAUILIA, pret. OAAUILI, etc., freq. of
61: : nite — to jest, to caress, to tickle someone.
102: Perhaps: oc-itla), perhaps there is something else. See Aco.
103: A kind of parasitic plant that grows on certain trees.
104: Formed from aca-ço-ye-amo or from açaçoquitla (for açaço- ac-aço-ye-amo. Perhaps not yet.
105: Perhaps not. Formed from aca-co-amo or from ac-aço-amo; by transposing the terms one obtains açocamo, which has the same meaning: acaçomo or açocamo neltilizthi in tlein ot-ilhuiloc (Car.), perhaps it is not the truth that which was said; acaçomo ye or yehuatl, perhaps it is not him; acaçomo iuh or iuhqui, perhaps wrongly, doubtless not as it should be; ma xinechmotla-popolhuili, in acaçomo yuhqui inic oniqui-cuilo (Car.), forgive me, perhaps I wrote it incorrectly. See Amo.
106: n. A kind of frog. RR. acail, cueyatl.
107: A partition of reeds used for catching fish. RR. acatl, cuextli (?).
111: Reed, aromatic fragrant plant that was placed on tombs (Clav.); perhaps a tobacco stalk. RR. acatl, yetl.
112: Pret. OACAYYECHICHIN: (for ni) — to suck aromatic plants. RR. acayetl, chichina.
113: n. v. sucking of aromatic plants. R. acayyechichina.
115: n. v. One who sucks aromatic plants. R. acayyechichina. Sucking.
116: Pret. OACAYOAC, v. n. to become covered with reeds; acayoa in milli, the field becomes filled and covered with reeds. R. acatl.
117: That which concerns the urethral canal, the penis, or the penis itself. In comp. tacayo (for to-acayo), our urethra, the urethra in general, the virile member; couatl iacayo, vulva, vagina; lit. of the woman her canal. R. acatl.
118: Knot of a reed. RR. acatl, ixtli.
119: Pret. OACAIXUAC, v. n. To become covered with reeds, speaking of a field. RR. acatl, ixua.
120: Pret. OACALACAN: to beach a ship or boat, to put it aground. RR. acalli, acana.
121: Pret. OACALAQUI: nino (for mno) — ni (for ni) — to embark. Nite — to embark someone. RR. acalli, aquia.
182: In no place, nowhere: acan ni-quitta, acan mic-nepanoa (Car.), I see him nowhere, I do not go with him at all. Followed by ma, acan serves to negate in an absolute manner: acan iuhqui nic-caqui, acan ma onic-cac in iuhqui intlachihual in tlatoque (Gar.), nowhere have I heard such things, no, nowhere have I heard that the great ones did such things. RR. a priv. can.
183: Not little, much; this expression seems to be a softened form of the word atzan, much, often; açan nino-mati, to esteem oneself highly, to be proud of oneself; açan üla ipan tic-malz, you will not esteem it for little. RR. a priv. çan.
184: Pret. OAГАН: to run aground, speaking of a ship. v. or nic — m (for mo) — to run aground, to beach, to pull a thing out of the water; to winnow, to clean grain, etc.; nic-acana in acalh, I run the ship aground. See Acazacana. RR. atl, ana.
186: Pret. OAGANTOCA: nino (for nino) — to be lying down, stretched out. RR. acana (?), onoc.
187: Reed hurdle, raft, boat, bark. RR. acatl, pepechtli.
192: Place where reeds grow. RR. acapitzactli, tla.
193: Rush, a kind of reed. RR. acatl, pitzactli.
195: Why yes, certainly. RR. amo, çaquema.
197: Pret. OACATIX, v.n. to grow, to increase, said of maize, near Quauhnahuac. R. acatl.
198: n. and v. One who pushes forward, grows; fig. chief, governor (Olm.). R. acatia.
199: Hook, fishhook. In comp. yacatepuco, its hook: topilli yacatepuco ie michmalo, fishhook for catching fish. RR. acatl, tepuztli.
200: n. Reed. Cal. name of a year and a day: ic omacatl xihuitl, year two reed; auh ça no ipan inin omoteneuh ce acatl xihuitl in mo-miquillico in Tenochtzin (Chim.), in this said year one reed (1363) died Tenochtzin; matlactli omei acatl ilhuitl, thirteenth day of the month, day thirteen reed. RR. atl, ca(??).
142: OR ACALLANEOUANI, n. v. Charter, freight, hire price of a vessel, passage fees. R. acallaneuia.
143: n. v. Freighter, charterer. R. acallaneuwia.
144: Pret. OACALLANEUI: # OR nin (for ni or nino) — to charter, to freight a ship. RR. acalli, tlaneuia.
145: n. v. Shipwreck; lit. rupture of the vessel. R. acallapani.
146: Pret. OACALLAPAN: n (for ni) — to suffer a shipwreck; to break apart, to be wrecked, speaking of a ship: nopan oacallapan, or without comp. otlapan in acalli, upon me (to my misfortune) the ship was wrecked. RR. acalli, tlapani.
148: Freight, hire price of a vessel. RR. acalli, tlaxtlauiloni.
149: Ship, bark, sloop, watercraft; lit. water building; uey acalli, large vessel. When accompanied by one of the following terms: cemmantiuilz, centettiuilz, pepexocatiuitz, quitzacutiuitz or lepeuhtiuitz, it means fleet. In comp. nacal (for no-acal), my ship. With the postpositions co, pan: acalco, in or on the vessel; acalco teachcauh, tiacheauh or tiacauh, captain of a vessel; acalco teichtacamicti, teichtacamictia or teichtacamictiani, corsair; acalco nino-tlalia or nite-tlalia, to embark or to embark someone; acalpan, on the ship; acalpan necalliztli or tlayecolitl, naval combat. RR. atl, calli.
150: Flank of a ship. RR. acalli, maitl.
151: ACALMOCUITLAUIANIT, or ACALMOCUITLAUIQUI, n. v. Sailor, seaman; lit. one who takes care of the ship. RR. acalli, cuitlahua.
152: OR ACALNEMACHILIANI, n. v. Pilot, sailor who holds the rudder, steers the vessel. R. acalnemachilia.
153: Pret. OACALNEMACHILI: # (for ni) — to steer, to navigate a vessel, to be its pilot. RR. acalli, nemachilia.
154: Pret. OACALO: nitla — to channel, to hollow out wood or something else.
156: Pret. OACALPAPANOC: n (for ni) — to sail for leisure, to amuse oneself. RR. acalli, pano.
157: Pret. OACALPATIOTI: # (for ni) — to charter a ship. RR. acalli, patotia.
158: Price, passage fees on a ship. RR. acalli, patiotl.
161: Ship's mast; lit. wood of the sails of the ship. RR. acalquachpantl, quauitl.
383: See ACTICAC, pret. OACTIGATCA or OACTICAYA: v. (for ni) — to be placed, sunk somewhere. Notech tla-actica, I owe a thing; itech tla-actica, he is a debtor. RR. aqui, ca or icac.
384: Pret. OACTIMOTECAC, v. n. To spread, to become known; tepan actimoteca, to spread everywhere, speaking of a rumor, a piece of news, etc.; nouian tepan actimoteca in iteyotzin Dios, the glory of God spreads everywhere; oc actimoteca in nitic, to have a clear conscience (tener sosegada la consciencia).
386: Pret. OACTINEN covered, sunk; iztacatitlan actinemi, he is dressed in white, he is immersed in white: v. (for ni) — to go dressed in white, he is sunk in white. RR. aqui, nemi.
387: Pret. OAGTILAZ: nitla or nic — to sink, press, tread down the earth. RR. actia, tlaça.
388: Much diminished, lost, destroyed, stolen. R. actitlaça.
389: Pret. OACTIUETZ: v. (for ni) — to fall unexpectedly into a hole, a barely visible hollow; to collapse, to settle, to compact, speaking of a construction; actiuetzi in tlapoalli, the count is badly done. RR. aqui, uetzi.
390: n. v. sinking. R. actiuetzi. Fall, collapse.
391: Pret. OACTIA: v. (for ni) — to interfere, to penetrate; tetlan n-actiuh, I meddle in an affair, I insinuate myself among people. R. aqui.
392: Pret. OACTOCA: v. (for ni) to be placed, sunk somewhere. Notech tla-actoc, I am indebted for a thing; itech tla-actoc, he is indebted for it. RR. aqui, onoc.
394: Undulating, swollen with waves. R. acuecueyotl.
395: Pret. OACUECUEYOTI: v. (for mo) — to be agitated, to form waves, speaking of water. R. acuecueyotl.
396: From acuecueyotl. Wave, billow, surge. — Name of the goddess of rain. See Cnaz-
398: Wave, billow, surge of the sea; the sea itself (Olm.): uey acueyotl, great wave. With the postposition pan: acueyopan, in the wave; cecem acueyopan, from wave to wave. RR. atl, cueitl.
399: Undulating, swollen, covered with waves. R. acueyotl.
402: Pret. OACUEZCOMAUI: nino (for mo) — to fall into a hole, into a well, etc. R. acuezcomatl.
403: Large aquatic lizard. RR. atl, cuetzpalin (?).
282: etc.: min (for nino) — to hurry, to hasten, to take the lead. Nitla — to be the first to do something (Par.). R. achtopa.
283: Pret. OACHTOPAITO: to say, to prophesy. RR. achtopa, itoa.
286: Pret. OAGIG: min (for nino) — to concentrate; noyollo m-aci, to think, to reflect, to consider; to recognize something, to verify it. N or non — to reach with the hand, to arrive at a place, to succeed, to live, to pass one's days, etc.; tetech ou tech n-aci, I have relations with a woman; notech act, she has relations with me; wech n-aci in tlaltecutli, I die; with the neg. atetech n-aci, to flee, to escape from someone; acaltica n-aci, to sail to the end of life; tepan n-aci, to surprise someone; teuan n-aci, to follow someone to the end; amotech aciz in amotlaxtlauil, you will have your wages; lit. your wages will come to you; inelhuayocan n-aci, to verify completely, to examine a matter thoroughly (see Necuvayorz); noyollo tech act, to feel keenly, to be moved by something; teitec ou teitic aci, it penetrates deeply, goes to the very core; uel aci, to come very opportunely; nouian aci in tawaca ou in iuelica, the smell spreads everywhere; ye non-ac, to approach a place, along the way; ye on-aci, the deadline has already passed, it has happened; ye itech non-aci, to draw near to something; pan n-aci, to succeed, to have good fortune; with the neg. anon-aci, to fail to reach something, not to arrive at one's goal, to live in want, in misery, etc. Nite or nonte — to catch someone who is fleeing, to equal, to seize, to take prisoner. In comp. nütlaquataci (for nitlaquati-aci), to come, to approach in order to eat. Rev. axilua ou axitia (Olm.). Pass. and impers. axihua (Par.) ou aciua, axoa (Olm.).
287: n. Place where one arrives, goal, end. In comp. #acian (for no-acian), my end, my finish; ona-cico in nacian, I am at the end, my end has come. R. aci.
288: Accomplished, perfect, full of qualities. R. aci.
289: Pret. VACICACAG: nitla — to understand, to grasp something perfectly, a matter, etc. RR. aci, caqui.
290: Pret. OACICAITTAG: nitla or nite — to understand, to know something perfectly, to see it clearly, to penetrate it. RR. aci, ta.
291: Pret. OACACICAMA: n (for #), nitla or nite — to know, to understand something perfectly.
293: Pret. OACICATEMO: nitla or nite — to search for something with great care. RR. aci, temoa.
299: One who accomplishes, perfects, reaches, approaches; tetlaliqui ac, ravager, despoiler in war, one who seizes the property of others; atetech acini, taciturn, unsociable, one who avoids people. Sometimes it is joined to the particle on: ye itech ona-im, one who approaches a place. R. aci.
300: Pret. OACITICATCA: # (for mo) — to be perfect, accomplished; m-acitica ou mo-cemaciticayehuatlin, it is perfect, accomplished. See Acrrzinorica. RR. aci, ca.
301: Pret. OACITINEN: anon — to live, to be in misery, in poverty. RR. aci, nemu.
404: Small lobster, crayfish, sea crayfish. RR. atl, cuicilin (?).
405: Plant whose leaves were used by priests to collect the blood they drew from themselves as penance (Clav.).
407: Or AFHECATL, n. Rain wind, sea wind: wei aehecatl, great sea wind.
409: Inconvenience, difficulty. RR. a priv., elewlizth.
410: n. Wineskin, utensil, vessel for carrying water. RR. atl, euatl, comitl. A type of bird. RR. ac-
411: In general, for words beginning with An, see A, etc. *AHUACAAZEITE, n. Oil extracted from the fruit of the avocado tree. RR. ahuacatl, azeite.
412: Oil extracted from the avocado pear. RR. ahuacatl, chiauallotl.
413: Field of avocado trees. RR. ahuacatl, milh.
414: n. Stew, dish made from avocado pears. RR. ahuacatl, mulli.
415: Or AUACATL, n. A type of drupe known as the avocado pear, fruit of the avocado tree; testicle.
417: Caviar (Clav.). The Mexicans made and still make great use of it (ahuautle). See AxayacarL.
418: (Don Carlos), illegitimate son of Neçahualpilli, whom Cortès installed at Tetzcuco after the death of Tecocoltzin (Aub.).
420: One of the twenty chiefs who founded Tenochtitlan (Clav.).
423: v. "In the pleasant water" (Clav.). The Spanish made Orizaba from it. RR. auia, apan.
302: Pret. OACITIUETZ: to quickly reach someone who is walking, to fall upon or reach the enemy, etc.; to scold, to quarrel with someone. Nitla — to chase; to seize, to snatch a thing swiftly, violently. RR. ac, ueta.
303: Pret. OACITIA: mo — to go approaching; noyollo m-acitiuh, to make sure of something, to go looking into a matter; lit. my spirit goes approaching; ye non (for ni-on) R. ac.
304: ACITLALIN, n. Drop of dew, dewdrop, water pearl (aljofar). RR. ail, citlalin. To approach a place.
305: Pret. OACITLAN: nic — to want something to come near; notech nic-acitlani, I allow, I want that to arrive, to reach me. See AXITLANI.
307: Pret. OACITZINOTICATCA, rev. of ACITICA: mo — to be perfect, accomplished; a totecuiyo m-acitzinotica or mo-cemacitzinotica in tlamatini, our lord is very wise; lit. perfect as a wise man.
310: Above, at the top: aco n-icac, to be up high, at the top; aco nitla-tlalia, to put a thing at the top, above; sometimes roof, summit; aco calli, the top of the house.
311: Probably, no doubt, perhaps: aço moztla ni-pixcaz (Par.), no doubt I will harvest tomorrow; aço moztla ni-huallaz, anoço quin huiptla (Gar.), perhaps I will come tomorrow or the day after; ago ueli, perhaps possible; aço ca ueli, perhaps it is not possible; aço amo, perhaps not; same meaning as acaçomo or açocamo: aço amo neltiliztli, perhaps it is not true, perhaps it is not the truth; ago ça or aço çan, perhaps, no doubt: aço çan ic tiquim-mocniuhti, perhaps you did something to have them as friends; aço çan te otic-cuic, perhaps you took it willingly, did you steal it; aço çan tic-mauizcauh, perhaps you left it out of fear; aço çan nen, perhaps uselessly, without profit, without reason; aço çan nouian, perhaps everywhere, on all sides; aço cana, perhaps somewhere; aço ye cana ?, perhaps already nearby: aço ye cana iz huitz, perhaps he is already near here, that is, he is perhaps there arriving; aço yuh, although, even though; ago ma? syn. of cuix? is it that? aço ma oticmo-machiti in ye om-axitico in tlatoani? (Car.), did you know how the governor has already arrived? tiqu-itoa aço ma oc azcentlamantin, entendiamos que erades de otra casta (Car.); aço quema, perhaps it is so; aço quen, about, approximately: aço quen macuilpa amo onic-cac missa, about five times I have not heard mass; aço quemmanian, perhaps sometimes: aço quemmanian oncan mo-cehuiquiuh, perhaps sometimes he will come to rest there.
313: Peace, calm; acomalli, acoallanlli, atlauelli ipan n-iaznequi (Olm.), I do not want to sow discord where there is peace. RR. a priv., coallanlli (?).
314: Eel, water serpent. RR. atl, coatl.
315: A kind of aquatic bird. RR. acoatl(?), tototl.
317: Perhaps not: açocamo, nopillane, neçahualizpan timo-çahua? (Pax.), perhaps, my son, have you not fasted during Lent? See ACAÇOMO.
318: Wakefulness, insomnia, absence of sleep: acochiztli nicno-chiualtia, to keep watch, to stay up through the night; lit. to make oneself wakeful. RR. a priv., cochiztli.
319: Pret. OACOCHOLO: ni or non — to leap into the air, to jump for joy. RR. aco, choloa.
321: A drink or potion used to speed up childbirth. See CAÇAZTIC. RR. acococo (?) çaçalic.
464: Not until now, not yet, not before, not earlier, not previously. See Aou. RR. a priv. ye.
465: In a bad place, at a bad moment, at an unfavorable time, in an unpropitious period. RR. a priv. yeccan.
468: Wickedness, vice, malice. RR. a priv. yecth.
469: Field of beans, of kidney beans. RR. ayecotli, cuemitl.
470: n. v. Intolerable thing. RR. a priv. yecoa.
471: Field of beans, of kidney beans.
476: pret. OAYECTLACHIUH: (for ri) — to act badly, to commit evil deeds. RR. Wickedness, vice, malice. ayecth, chua.
477: n. v. Thing badly done, action contrary to good. R. ayectlachiua.
478: Evil, vice, everything contrary to good; ayectli nic-chiua, to do harm, to commit evil deeds. RR. a priv. yectli.
480: Negligent, lazy; ayel nettaliatli, discord, disunion, enmity, the state of persons who cannot stand the sight of each other; aymel motta, enemies; ayel leilla or teittani, misanthrope, enemy of people; lit. one who does not want to see them; ayel teittalizth, misanthropy; ayel tenotza or ayellatoa (for ayel-tlatoa), taciturn, one who speaks to no one. See Ez.
481: adj. Bad, wicked, perverse, one who does not listen (Olm.). RR. ayel, tlacaqui.
483: pret. OAYEQUITO: me — to speak ill of others. RR. ayectli, itoa.
525: One of the commanders of the army of Tlaxcala, allied with Cortès (Clav.).
526: Gourd vessel, squash used in medicine (Bét.). RR. ayoth, …..(?).
528: Gourd, squash, melon. RR. ayotl, tetl.
529: or AYUTIA, pret. OAYOTI, etc. to wet, to soak something, to add water to it. R. ayoa.
531: or AYUTL, n. Turtle; juice, sap, broth (Car.). In comp. nayouh (for no-ayouh), my turtle. R. atl.
532: which paid to Mexico a tribute of gold dust (Clav.).
533: Gourd, squash; a kind of melon: mitla — (B. Diaz); chichic ayotli (Bét.), bitter squash used as a remedy. R. atl (?).
535: Pret. OAYOTOGAC: squash seeds. Impers. ayotoco, one sows — to sow squash seeds. RR. ayotl, toca.
536: Quadruped; a kind of lizard covered with scales (Clav.). RR. ayotli, tochtli.
538: dim. of ayotl. Small gourd, small squash; ayotonth xoxouhcaqualon, a kind of cucumber that can be eaten raw.
539: Village situated on the southern shore of Lake Chalco (Clav.).
540: Pret. OAYOTZINCUEP: nino — to flutter, to turn, to climb (boltear ó trepar al modo de España). RR. ayotl (?), cuepa.
542: n. Squash harvested before maturity, dried and kept for use in seasoning stews. RR. ayotli, tzoyacatl.
504: Coarseness, rudeness, violence. RR. a priv. yocoxcayotl.
505: Restless, troubled, agitated. RR. a priv. yocoxcanemini.
507: n. Ugliness, deformity, rudeness. RR. ayocoxqui, nexiliztli.
508: or AYUCUXQUI, adj. Coarse, rude, violent, hot-headed. RR. a priv. yocoxqui.
510: pret. OAYOICHIC: to shave someone cleanly, to shave well. RR. ... (?), ichiqui.
513: or AYOLLO, adj. Ignorant, unskilled, coarse, without talent; ayollo tlapaltiliztli, weakness, cowardice, poltroonery, inconstancy; ayollo tlapalte, weak, without heart, who has no courage. RR. a priv. yollotl.
514: Rudeness, coarseness, clumsiness. RR. a priv. yollocayotl.
515: Lack of courage. RR. a priv. yollochicaualiz tli.
516: Gulf, cove, inlet, or recess in a bay. RR. atl, yollotl, co.
518: A sort of color between violet and orange. RR. ayotl (?), pal.
519: n. Amethyst, precious stone. RR. ayopalli, teuilotl.
520: Not twice: ayoppa in tlacatioa ou piltioa, one is not born twice. See Tlacani and Oppa. RR. a priv. oppa.
522: pret. OAYOQUIXTI: nitla to extract, to draw out the juice, water, broth, etc., to express,
545: Easily, without difficulty; ayoui mo-chiua, easy, that which is done without effort, without difficulty. RR. a priv. youi.
546: pret. oAyour: nitla — to heat, to warm something with one's breath, to breathe.
549: or AYUXUCHITL, n. Squash flower. RR. ayotl, xochtl.
550: n. Squash flower. RR. ayoxochitl, quilitl.
551: Gulf, inland water. RR. atl, itetl.
555: pret. OAITI: Ain (for nino) — to do, to commit; Guardian of a courtyard. anonezcalicayo nin-aitia, to commit acts of foolishness, recklessness, or extravagance. Rev. of A.
556: Gulf, indentation in a bay; aitic calacqui, entered into a gulf, or into the interior of the sea. RR. atl, inc.
557: Fearful, skittish, timid, frightened by the slightest thing.
561: Not thus, otherwise, askew, wrongly: ayuh nite-caqui, to penetrate the secret intention or disguised thought of someone; ayuh tlattalh, penetrated, guessed; ayuh ca teyollo, unexpectedly, by surprise, it is against someone's expectation (subitamente). RR. a priv. yuh.
562: Error, fault: intla itla ayuh-cayotl or achiuhcayotl tocon-chiuaz, cuix amo titla-tzacuiltiloz? (Par.), when you commit some fault, will you not be punished? R. ayuh.
563: or AYUIANYO, adj. Extended, without measure, without limit, without end, etc. RR. a priv. yuianyo.
444: pret. OAYATI: v. (for nino) — to be a lord, honored, esteemed; lit. to have, to wear a manta (Olm.). R. ayatl.
445: Manta, garment, fine cloth of cotton or maguey (ayate).
449: Daughter of Teçogomoc, ruler of Azcapuçalco, who married Huitzilihuitl, second king of Mexico (Clav.). RR. ayauül, cihuatl.
451: pret. OAYAUHYOTI, v. n. To drizzle.
452: pret. OAYAUHYOTITICATOA: v. (for mo) — to drizzle, to be in the process of drizzling. RR. ayauhyotia, ca.
453: n. Place planted with pine trees. RR. ayauhquauitl, tla.
454: Pine, white cedar. RR. ayaul, quauitl.
455: pret. OAYAUHTIMANCA, v. n. To drizzle, to mist. RR. ayaui, mani.
456: pret. OAYAUH, v. n. To drizzle. R. ail.
457: n. Fog, mist, drizzle, haze. Fig. mixtli, pucth, ayauitl qui-molonaltico (Olm.), he came to put forward a new doctrine. R. ayaui.
458: also AYAXCAN, adv. With difficulty, with effort, slowly: ayaxcan qui-chiua, it is slow, tardy, it is done slowly; ayaxcan nicno-nextiha in noneuhca (Car.), with difficulty I obtain my food; in iquac nech-nananquilia in nonamic, in noço ayaxcan tlein qui-chiua, nonno-tequipachotiuh, nic-ahua (Car.), when my wife answers me or does something slowly, I get angry, I scold her. RR. aya, axcan.
459: Soft, slow; tayaxcanyo (for ti-ayaxcanyo), you are slow, inactive. R. ayaxcanyotl.
460: With sluggishness, nonchalance, slowly. RR. ayaxcanyotl, ca.
463: sync. of amo. Never, at no time: aic oni-tlauan, I have never gotten drunk. Loc. adv. aic caui, always, incessantly, perpetually; aic cemelle, without peace, never with tranquility; aic cenca, rarely: aic cenca mo-chua, that happens rarely; aic ma quemman or aic quemman, never, at no time; aic quen, aic quem mo-chiua, that does not vary, it is immutable, unalterable, eternal.
484: An unbearable, intolerable thing; ayeualiztli ipan nic-mati, to regard something as unbearable and very painful. RR. priv. eua.
485: Pret. OAILI: nitla — to work for someone; freq. nitetlatla —; tetlatla-ailia, he works for others, he is a porter. Rev. of Av.
486: A kind of plant (?). Fig. ützmolini in ailitl (Olm.), he is rich, he has what he needs; lit. the plant grows. RR. atl(?), ill.
487: With activity, actively, with occupation, laboriously; atle ailiztica, with idleness, without eagerness in anything. RR. œhztli, ca.
488: n. v. Activity, action, occupation, work; atle ailizili, leisure, idleness. R. ay.
489: Diminution, lowering of the water; uei aloulith, recession of the sea. RR. atl, iloti.
492: Active, hardworking; muchè or much uel aini, perfect, accomplished, who does everything well. R. ay. AYO! interj. To call someone, hey! ho!
493: Watery, juicy, having sap; corrected, amended. R. ayotl.
494: Pret. oayoac, etc., v. n. (Par.), to get wet, to fill with water. Impers. One drinks; usually accompanied by the impers. tlaqualloa, one eats; ayoa tlaqualloa, to have a wedding feast; lit. one drinks, one eats. R. atl.
496: (for AYOAGH-GHIAUAGAYOTL, etc.), n. Oil from seeds, from kernels. Also said: ayouachazeite.
498: or AYOUACHTLI, n. Pumpkin seeds. RR. ayotl, achtli.
499: Pret. OAYOTI: nitla — to wet, to soak something in water (aguar algo, Olm.). R. ayoa.
501: Already not: ayoc ni-pinaua, to no longer have any shame; ayoc axcan, with difficulty, with effort; ayocac, rev. ayocacatzintli, he is no longer there, he is not seen; ayoc campa, already nowhere, from nowhere; ayoc tle or tlei, nothing more, not any further, that is all, it is finished; ayoc tle monectoc, monequi, motemachia or tlacotli tlatquitl, one lacks nothing, there is an abundance of everything.
503: Already not: ayocmo ommo-caqui, rebellious, disobedient, insubordinate, who does not listen; ayocmo nocon-caqui, to be proud, rebellious, arrogant, incorrigible; ayocmo uecauh, shortly after, soon; lit. there is not long to wait. See Aocmo.
424: Or AUITZOTL, n. (Clav.). Amphibious quadruped that lives in the hot lands.
426: Pret. oax or oaxqui (Olm.): (for ni) to do, to make; much uel ay, he does everything well; tle tay? (for ti-ay), what are you doing? atle ay, he does nothing, one who is idle, lazy; ye n-ay, to practice, to exercise. Nitla — to do something outwardly; nitic nitla-ay, to meditate, to reflect, to act inwardly; freq. nitlatla-ay, to do something often. Sometimes the pronoun tla or itla is separated from the verb: itla n-ay, I do something; in itla ay, one who does something. Rev. ailia, ailia or aitia (Olm.). Pass. aihua (Par.).
427: Sync. of ayamo, adv. Not yet, not yet: aya x tiuitz? or cenca aya te? You are not yet here? you are not back yet? aya temo, it is indigestible, it has not yet gone down; aya temoliztli, indigestion; aya cuel, too early, betimes, before time (temprano); aya quemman, too early, prematurely, betimes; aya quimati, ignorant, stranger, unlearned, minor, very young, who still knows nothing; aya quimatilizth or aya tlachialiali, undeveloped intelligence or memory, like that of a child, of the ignorant, etc.; aya tle, nothing yet; aya tle quiqua, he is fasting, or one who is fasting, that is, who has not yet eaten anything; aya uel or ueli, not yet well, not quite; aya uel cexiuhtia cavallo conetl, a foal that is not quite a year old; in aya, before: in aya n-ac, before I was born; in aya titleme, before we were; in aya yaque, before they were.
428: None, no, nobody, nothing: ceme, none of them; ayac tlacatl, no person; ayac, there is nobody, it is deserted, uninhabited; ayac oualla, nobody came, he did not come; ayac çan moyocuyaz, nobody will dare to do it; ayac ipan nite-mati, to despise someone, to hold them in no esteem; ayac ixco tlachia, proud, arrogant, presumptuous; lit. he sees nobody before him; he considers only himself; ayac yuan, or yuiui, nobody, nothing is comparable to him; ayac yuui or thuihui imic chicahuac, none equals him in strength; ayac ma, none, nothing; ayac maca, none who does not: ayac maca mitz-irimati, there is no one who does not know him; in ayac, without. Plur. ayaque, none, they are absent. See Ac.
429: Pret. OAYACACHILHUI: (for ni) — to play the drum (Par.); serves as rev. to the following.
430: Pret. OAYACACHO: ni (for ni) — to play the drum. R. ayacachtli.
431: Pret. OAYACACHQUETZ: (ni), to play the drum. RR. ayacachtli, quetza.
432: n. A drum, (sonajas hechas à manera de dormideras), a musical instrument (for ni) consisting of an oval vessel pierced with small holes and filled with small stones. This vessel, fixed at the end of a stick, was used for dancing (Clav.).
433: Nowhere yet, in no place: ayacan quiça, he or she goes nowhere, does not go out; ayacan nicno-ittilia in tlatoani (Par.), I see the governor nowhere yet. RR. aya, can.
434: Not yet a little: ayachi quimati, disturbed, troubled, senseless; ayachi quimaulizthi, disturbance, trouble, madness. RR. aya, achi.
435: A kind of very large beans or kidney beans.
440: Never until now: ayaic nino-cocoa in ye iæquich cahuitl ni-nemi, until now I have never been sick in all my life. See Inrcacayaro.
442: Not yet, not yet: ayamo temo, it has not yet gone down, said of food, it is on the stomach; ayamo temoliztli, indigestion. Loc. adv. ayamo cenca, not yet well: ayamo cenca quen nocom-mati, I do not yet feel it, I do not notice it; ayamo imman or ayamo oncan, it is not yet time, it is not the moment: ayamo imman in ht-euazque, ca oc tlatlayouatoc, it is not time for us to get up, it is still dark; ayamo oncan in uiloaz, it is not yet time to leave; ayamo niman, not quickly, not right away; ayamo uecauh, shortly before, not long ago; ayamo uecauh in omixiuh in nonamic, AYACAN QUIEA, ayamo uel m-eua, oc noma uetztoc (Car.), it was not long ago that my wife gave birth, she does not go out, she cannot yet get up, she is still lying down; ayamo uel or ueli, it is not yet possible, not quite: ayamo uel ni-pati, oc noma nino-cocotinemi, I am not quite recovered, I am still sick; in ayamo, before: in ayamo mo-chiua, before that happens; in ayamo yoaz, before nightfall; in ayamo quh pehua in cemanahuatl (Par.), before the world began.
443: n. A mantle, rough and coarse garment. RR. ayatl, popolli (?).
202: to the S.E. of Chalco (Clav.). RR. acatl, tlan.
204: First, firstly: ma acattopa, nopiltzine, xictla-popolhui in mo-tecocolicauh, auh çatepan où quin çatepan timo-yolewiuiz (Par.), first, my son, forgive your enemy, then you will confess. See Yacarro.
209: a place near the lake of Tetzcuco, also called Mexicaltzinco (Clav.).
210: A place covered with brush. RR. acauall, tla.
211: Dry brushwood, uncultivated land, fallow field. RR. a priv. caua (??).
212: A type of aquatic insect, mite.
214: Reservoir, water basin. RR. ail, caxutl.
215: dim. of acaxitl. Trough, small reservoir.
218: Pret. OACECEYOAC, etc. v. n. To become overgrown with weeds, said of a neglected field. R. acecentli, etc.
221: Pret. OACELAN: someone. RR. acelin, ana.
644: Arms of a city. RR. altepetl, tlauizth.
645: n. of a city, a borough, etc. RR. altepetl, ca.
646: Pret. OALTEPETZINTI: (for nt) — to found a city, a state. RR. altepetl, tzintia.
647: Founder of a city, a state. R. altepetzintia.
648: of a city, a state. R. altepetzintia.
649: Townsman, inhabitant of a city, a country. In comp. naltepeuacauh (for no-altepeuacauh), my fellow townsman. See Ava. R. altepetl. Dependency, territory. Foundation of a
650: Pret. OALTI: # Where nin (for ni or nino) — to bathe oneself, to get into the water; one also finds nim (for nimo). Nite — to bathe someone; to do a favor, to grant an advantage, speaking of a merchant; to sacrifice, to immolate victims to idols, to make offerings. Vila or nice — to wash something; c-altiz in ütlacayo (Av.), he will wash his body. Impers. nealhilo, one bathes, everyone bathes. R. atl.
652: Sheet of paper. RR. amatl, aaztli.
655: Pret. OAMACAC: nite — to give someone a drink, to offer water to someone. RR. atl, maca.
656: Bookshop, stationery shop, miter, paper cone. RR. amatl, calli.
657: Pret. OAMACALTI: to put a paper miter on someone. R. amacalli.
660: Sweet, imperceptible; amachiztli octli, sweet wine. RR. a priv., machiztia.
785: Abyss, deep water; amo cenca amictlan, shallow sea, not very deep. RR. ail, lan.
786: pret. OAMILI: nite— to hunt for someone, for others. R. ami.
788: Land, irrigated field. With the postposition pampa: toward the South; amilpampa eecal, south wind. RR. ail, maille.
789: n. v. Hunting ground. R. amilia.
790: Egg of the fish called amilotl. RR. amilotl, tetl. amilpampa, on the side of the irrigable lands.
793: adj. v. Coarse, rude, unintelligent. RR. a priv. mimati.
794: Wave, billow, swell. RR. ail, minu- liui(?).
795: God of fishing, particularly venerated at Cuitlahuac, in the lake of Chalco, who is none other than the god Opochtli (Clav.).
797: Pret. OAMIN: min (for mino) — to experience pain, suffering, to have diarrhea after having eaten something raw and drunk water; m-amina, he has diarrhea, one who has diarrhea (doliente de camaras). RR. al, mina.
798: n. v. Hunter: amini itzcuinth, greyhound, hunting dog. R. ami.
801: Pret. OAMIC: to die of thirst. Micn (for ni) — to be thirsty, to thirst for something, for spiritual things, to desire them ardently. Impers. amicoa, one dies of thirst, all die of thirst. Rev. amiquitia, amiquilia or amiquilitzinoa. RR. atl, miqui.
802: In an immortal manner. RR. amiquiliztli, ca.
804: Or AMIQUIZTLI, n. v. Immortality. RR. a priv. miqui.
82: where AÇACANI, n. v. (açacan). R. açaca. n (for ni) — to carry water. Water carrier.
83: pret. oAÇACAc, freq. of Açaca: n (for ni) — to carry, transport a large amount of water.
84: Water carrier, one who does it as a trade. R. açaçaca.
85: Rush, gladiolus, a type of reed.
87: A place planted with rushes, with reeds. RR. acaçacatl, tla.
88: n. Couch grass. RR. acatl, cacauitzth.
89: (for aca-aço-ayuc or aço-ayac), adj. Perhaps nobody, none: nino-matia acaçayac, açayac nech-palehuiz, I thought that perhaps no one would help me; açayac in momiquizpan mitz-palehuiz (Par.), perhaps no one will help you at death.
90: n. Diving bird, an amphibious bird resembling a crow. RR. atl, cacalotl.
91: n. v. One who drinks from the hollow of his hand. R. acacampazxoa.
92: pret. OACACAMPAXO: — to drink water from the hollow of the hand. RR. atl, n (for ni) camatl, paxoa (?).
93: n. v. The act of drinking from the hollow of the hand. R. acacampaxoa.
94: n. Coarse straw. RR. aca, çacatl.
95: (for acach-chapulin), n. A type of grasshopper. RR. acachatl, chapulin.
97: A place near Mexico where, according to Sahagun, Cortés would have had a meeting with King Quauhtemotzin and the Mexican nobles. RR. acatl, chinamitl, co.
98: Basket made of reeds. RR. acatl, chiquiuitl.
99: First, firstly. See Acarro, Acarropa. R. achto.
100: One of the twenty Aztec chiefs who founded Tenochtitlan (Clav.).
584: pret. OAIZNEG: nitla — to want to work, to do, to act; with the priv. atla-ainequi (Olm.), he wants to do nothing, he is lazy. RR. ay, nequi.
585: ALAUAC or ALAZTIC, adj. v. Slippery, that slips away like a fish, soap, etc. R. alaua.
586: Pret. OALAUH: to escape. Vite — to anoint, grease, rub someone. nin (for nino) — to slip. Tla — in ichantzinco in Dios, la yelesia de Dios es reverenciada (Olm.).
588: Mat of rushes. RR. alauacatl, petlatl.
589: Rush; lit. slippery reed. RR. alauac, acatl.
590: See ALacric. *ALCALDEYOTL, n. Office, dignity, jurisdiction of an alcalde.
591: n. Gourd, elongated squash used to extract the sap of the agave.
593: (?) n. Scrotum, testicle sac, membrane enclosing the testicles. In comp. yaloyo, his scrotum; yaloyo ompa m-ihiotia, his scrotum swells, is swollen.
594: Pret. OALPICHI: ne — to wet someone by squirting water from the mouth. Nitla — to water, sprinkle something. RR. atl, ilpitza.
595: Entrance, city gate. RR. altepetl, calacoayan.
596: The middle, the center, or in the heart of the city. RR. altepetl, yollotl, co.
597: Pret. OALTEPEITTAC: % (for ni) — to see, visit, travel through towns and cities. RR. altepetl, itta.
598: Hamlet, countryside near the city; peasant; pl. altepemame. RR. altepetl, maitl.
599: Fields of the city, communal property. RR. altepetl, milli.
600: Or ALTEPENANYOTL, n. Capital, metropolis, mother city, principal town. RR. altepetl, nanyotl.
601: Neighborhood, border of a city or country. RR. altepetl, nauac.
602: n. Laws, ordinances of a state. RR. altepetl, nauatilli.
765: of the 2nd person plural. You. Joins verbs with the personal pronouns ni and ti: na- mech, tamech, amech; namech-mahuizpoloa, I dishonor you; tamech-mahuizpoloa, we dishonor you; amech-mahuizpoloa, he dishonors you or they dishonor you. Idiom: namech-ahuili in amotatzin (Par.), I scolded your father; lit. I scolded you your father; namechim-mictili in amototolhuan, I killed your hens; lit. I killed you your hens.
766: Officer in charge of etiquette at court (Clav.).
772: n. Fountain, spring. With the postposition co: ameyalco, in the fountain, or the fountain. RR. atl, meya.
773: Spring, fountain. In comp.: yameyallo, its source; yameyallo in cemicac yolztl, source of life. R. ameyalli.
775: Injustice, wickedness, wrong. RR. a priv. melauacachiualizth.
776: Unjustly, wrongfully, wickedly. RR. amelaualizth, ca.
777: Injustice, wrong, wickedness. RR. a priv. melaualizth.
782: Pret. oan: to hunt. Pass. and impers. amiua (Olm.).
784: Pret. oamictinen: to ardently desire something, like someone (ni for n) — to hunt, to go hunting; mic — to desire, as one who is dying of thirst; nic-amictinemi in qualh, I desire virtue, I thirst for good. RR. amiqui, nemi.
604: 5. v. Support, protector of a city, of a state, one who enlarges it, develops it, and serves its interests. RR. altepetl, paleuia.
605: Foundation of a borough, a city, a state. RR. altepetl, peualtiztli.
607: : n (for ni) — to mark the boundaries of a country, a city, etc. RR. altepequaxochtli, quetza.
608: Boundaries of a country, a city. RR. altepetl, quaxochtli.
610: City that is surrounded by walls. RR. altepetl, tenamitl, ca.
613: Small rampart gate. RR. altepetenamitl, coyoctli (?).
614: Pret. OALTEPETENANQUETZ: n (for ni) — to set the boundaries, to raise the walls of a city. RR. altepetenamitl, quetza.
617: Pret. OALTEPETEPANQUETZ: n (for ni) — to fix, to determine the boundaries of a city, of a state. RR. altepetepantli, quetza.
620: Public work, public service. RR. altepetl, tlatequipanolizтли.
621: Borough, city, state, king, sovereign; cecem altepetl, each state, each city, in each city; uei altepetl, large city, province; in altepetl çam ütlan ca in occe altepetl, borough neighboring another; altepetl itech poui, bordering state; uei altepetl ipan tlacatl, city-dweller, inhabitant of a large city; altepetl ipa or ipan, from country to country, from city to city; cecem altepetl ipan, in or through each city, etc.; altepetl ipapana, from city to city, from country to country; ima, icxi in altepetl, suburb, quarter of a city; lit. the hands, the feet of the city. In comp. naltepeuh (for no-altepeuh), my country; taltepeuh, our city, our country. With the postp. pan: altepepan or altepepa, from city to city, from country to country; cecem altepepan, in each state, in each city, etc.; taltepepan (for to-altepepan), in our city. RR. atl, tepetl.
622: n. man who is not in holy orders. RR. altepetl, tlacatl. City-dweller, layman.
343: Pret. OACOLEUILI: nitetla — to threaten someone, to raise one's arm to strike. R. acoleua.
344: peoples who came to Anahuac toward the end of the 12th century and settled at Tetzcuco (Clav.).
345: State of Anahuac, capital Tetzeuco (Clav.). RR. ail, Colhuacan.
346: Acolhua chief who settled at Azcapugçalco, under Xolotl (Clav.).
347: n. Shoulder, muscle, by ext. arm. In comp. tacol, his/her shoulder; tacol (for to-acol), our arm(s); lit. half of the arm. With the postposition pan: shoulder, the shoulder in general; centlacol tacol, the tacolpan (for to-acolpan), on the shoulders.
348: to the north of Tetzcuco (Olm., Clav.).
351: Biceps of the arm. In comp. tacolnacayo (for to-acolnacayo), our biceps of the arm, the fleshy part of the arm in general. RR. acolli, nacayotl.
353: Shoulder, muscle. In comp. tacolteuh (for to-acolteuh), our shoulder, our muscles, the shoulder, the muscles in general. RR. acolli, tetl.
354: or ACOLTZUNTLI, n. Hairs of the shoulder. In comp. tacoltzonyo (for to-acoltzonyo), hairs that are on the shoulders. RR. acolli, tzontli.
357: Pret. OACOMAN: nin (for nino) — to become troubled, agitated; m-acomana, (the sea; the crowd) is agitated, becomes troubled, etc. Nite — to stir up, agitate others. Nitla — to overthrow, destroy something; tla-acomana in tlatoani (Olm.), the governor ruins the country. Impers. neacomanalo, one revolts, one rises up, the crowd stirs, becomes agitated, etc. RR. aco, mana.
358: Pitcher, vessel used to hold water. RR. atl, comitl.
359: Upward, from above: acopa itztiuh tzontli, hair in disarray, rising into the air; in tlein amo etic, in iuhqui tletl, acouic itztiuh, acopa tlamatiuh, that which, like fire, is not heavy rises into the air; not to be confused with wiccopa, copauic, toward, against: çan tuhquin cochitleualli temictli ipan tic-matiique in iz tlaticpac ic pacoa, in totech monequi ye acouic ilhuicac-copauic tocon-itztiltiazque in totlaelehuiliz (Car.), we must consider the pleasures of this world as dreams and direct our desires toward heaven. Acopa is often joined to a verb; see the following words. RR. aco, pa.
360: Pret. OACOPAITTAC: nite — to look at someone who is above. Nitla — to look at something that is in the air. RR. acopa, itta.
361: Pret. OACOPATLACHIX: n (for ni) — to look upward. RR. acopa, tlachia.
362: Pret. OACOPIN: nitla — to open, to dig a canal, a channel. RR. atl, copina.
363: Pret. OACOQUECHILI: niletla — to overcharge someone for something, to sell too dearly, to raise the price. RR. acoquetza.
364: Pret. OACOQUETZ: M (for mo) — to rise, to lift up, to surge, speaking of an object, the waves of the sea, etc.; m-acoquetza in patiotl, the price increases, rises. Mitla to raise, to increase the task, the work, to overcharge, to raise the price of a thing. RR. aco, quetza.
365: Pret. OAGOQUETZTIA: M (for mo) — to keep growing, to increase, to rise. R. acoquetza.
366: Pret. OAGOQUIZ, n (for ni) — to climb, to rise in dignity, to grow; to gain, to make a profit; in axcan nic-caqui in n-acoquiçaya (Olm.), now I hear that I am advancing. Nite — to elevate someone (Olm.). RR. aco, quiça.
367: n. v. Elevation, gain, profit, advantage, etc. R. acoquiça.
368: Pret. OAGOQUIXTI: nite — to elevate, to raise someone up. Mitla — to lift, to carry a thing upward. RR. aco, quixtia.
369: Pret. past. OACOQUIXTILI: ninote or nicno — to elevate someone, to bring them out of obscurity; oquim-acoquixtili in tlatoani, the governor elevated him, raised him up. R. acoquixtia.
370: adj. and n. v. Elevated, highly placed; one who has achieved gain or profit. R. acoquiça.
371: Pret. OACOTLAZ: to console, to recover, to take courage again. Nite — to console, to raise up someone, to give them courage. nino (for nino) — to console oneself, to recover.
373: Pret. OACOTLACHIX: n (for ni) — to look upward. RR. aco, tlachia.
374: n. v. Consolation; teyollo acouechihztli, contemplation. See Acouetzizтli. R. acouetzi.
375: Pret. OACOUETZ: N (for ni) — to become calm, to console oneself; acouetzi in noyollo, I contemplate; lit. my heart is at peace; iyollo acouetzi, he is in contemplation, his heart is calm, tranquil.
377: n. v. teyollo acouetziliztli, contemplation. R. acouetzi.
378: Light, not burdensome, causing no grief; consoled. R. acouetzi.
379: Upward, in the air: acouic nic-tlachia, to look upward; acouic nonte-itta, consolation, calm; to look upon the one who is above. See Acopa. RR. aco, uic.
380: n. Alga, seaweed (algae that grows in water). RR. atl, icpalli.
381: One who places himself among, enters, penetrates; tetlan aqui, one who is intermingled, who seeks to seduce a woman. R. aqui.
382: Pret. Oaori: nitla — to introduce, to interpose a thing. Nitetla — to incorporate someone, to have them received and admitted into a society. R. aqui.
63: etc.: nie — to make gestures, grimaces to frighten someone. RR. aauilia, mauhtia.
64: Or AHAUILNEMI, pret. OAAUILNEN, loc.: n (for ni) — to live in pleasures, joy, debauchery, lust. RR. aauihia, nemi.
65: Or AHAUILNEMILIZTLI, n. v. Debauchery, lust. R. aauilnemi.
66: Or AHAUILPAN, n. Place of recreation, pleasure, enjoyment. RR. aauia, pan.
67: Or AHAUILTIA, pret. OAAUILTI, etc., freq. of AAUILTIA: min (for nino) — to walk, to recreate oneself, to amuse oneself, to pass the time pleasantly; m-aauilta, he or she recreates, takes pleasure, leads a merry life; said especially of a courtesan. Vite — to provide joy, pleasure to someone.
70: Wing tips resembling knives (cuchillas de las alas de las aves). RR. aaztli, uitztli.
71: Or AHAUIXCA, adv. Pleasantly, joyfully. RR. aauia, ca.
72: The act by which one succeeds in knowing something perfectly. R. aaci.
73: pret. OAaxIx, freq. of axixA: min (for nino) — to urinate, to soil oneself with fear. Nite — to soil someone.
75: Soiled, covered in filth. R. aaxixth.
78: AQUI or AQUIN? interrogative pronoun. Who? Which one? ac axcaua? or aqui axca? ac tlaiquihua? or aqui tlatqui? who is the master? ac oc mitz-paleuiz? who then will help you? aquin in? (Par.), who is this one? aquin on? (Par.), who is that one? aqu yuhqui 0? who is like that one? It is often joined to the personal pronouns nehuatl, tehuatl, yehuatl, etc.: ac tehuatl? who are you? ac tehuantin? (Car.), who are we? ac amehuatin? who are you (pl.)? ac ye? ac yehuatl? aquehuatl or aqueuatl? which (among them)? or who is that one? ac ye in oc cenca qualli? which of them is the best? ac ye in oc cenca tic-tlaçotla? which do you like better? whom do you prefer? ac yehuail 1? who is this one? ac nel? who then? ac nel yaz? who then will go? Plur. aquique? aquique yehuantin? who are those ones? amaquique (for an-aquique) or, by interposing the pronoun an, acamique? (Car.), who are you (pl.)? which among you? or whoever among you? actique? (Car.), who are we? aquique onech-temoque? (Par.), who are those who sought me? Preceded by in, this pronoun ceases to be interrogative: in aquin nech-notza (Par.), he who calls me; in aquique (Olm.), those who.
79: is used with the personal pronouns ni, ti, a, to express being: in aya n-ac, before I was; and with the privative a to signify being absent: anac, I am absent; atac, you are absent; ayac, he is absent; ataque, we are absent; aamaque, you (pl.) are absent; ayaque, they are absent.
80: Someone, anyone; plur. acame. Used only for persons (Par.): aço aca amehuatin or aço aca ceme in amehuatin qu-ittazque in tein taltepepan mo-chihuaz (Par.), perhaps someone among you will see what will happen to our country.
81: Pret. OAÇACAC: water. RR. atl, çaca.
262: dim. of acur. Very little, almost nothing.
264: dim. of acui. A little, very little, a very small thing, somewhat: in ye achitzin omo-2cali in noconeton, ça niman omicno-maquili in nahuitzin (Par.), as soon as my child had grown a little, I entrusted him to my aunt; ye achitzin mohuel-mali in cocoxqui (Par.), the sick person is feeling somewhat better. See Quenrez.
265: or ACHITON, adv. dim. of acur. Somewhat, very little; çan achiton, in very small quantity.
267: One of the Aztec chiefs who founded Tenochtitlan (Clav.). — King of Colhuacan.
268: A moment, an instant: çan achitonca or cenca çan achitonca, very little time, in very little time; ca amo huel taxca in tlalticpac axcaitl, ca çan achitonca, ca can cuel achic ti-pieltilo (Gar.), the goods of this world are not ours, we keep them only for an instant; ye cuel achitonca in oni-hualla (Car.), it has been a good while since I arrived; oc or oc cuel achitonca, soon, in a moment; oc achitonca tihualmo-cuepaz (Par.), you will return in an instant. See Acuic. RR. achi, cauitl.
270: adv. A moment, an instant: çan achitzinca, very little time; quin on-acico, auh ye cuel tinechm-ihualia, ma ça oc achitzinca iz non-ye (Gar.), I have barely arrived and already you send me away, let me stay just a brief instant. See Acmic. RR. achi, cauitl.
271: pret. oAcmiun: titla — to do something illicit, which is not permitted. RR. a priv., chiua.
272: pret. AGHIUH: # (for ni) — to make cacao, to prepare a drink. RR. atl, chiua.
274: Illicit, not permitted, which ought not to be done. R. achiua.
275: n. A bad action, something wrong, etc.: in piltonth in iquac quemmanian achiuhcayotl ou aiuhcayotl con-ailia in nantli, in tatli, cuix amo tlatzacuiltilo? (Gar.), when a child does something wrong toward his mother or his father, is he not punished? R. achiua.
276: Seed, pip, grain. In comp. iachyo (from the unused achyotl), its seed, the seed of the fruit. It also means elder brother, superior, servant. In comp. nach (for no-ach), my elder brother; teach, someone's servant. See Acucauurr. R. achto.
277: ACHTOPA or ACHTOTIPA, adv. and adj. First, firstly, before, first (masc.), first (fem.), etc.: achto or achtopa n-iauh, to go first, to take the lead, to anticipate, to go ahead; achto nociauh, my first wife; achtopa tlacaxinachtin, the first man and the first woman; lit. first seed of men; achto neteochiualiztli, the first hour; oc ye achto or achtopa, first of all, above all.
279: dim. of Acutut. Great-grandfather. In comp. teachton, someone's great-grandfather.
805: adj. v. Parched with thirst. R. amiqui.
810: Happy, wealthy; who does not know himself; amirimati itlaqual, one who eats well, who lives sumptuously.
811: amo, iximau. v. Country, hunting ground.
812: Water lion, amphibious quadruped (Clav.). RR. atl, mizth.
813: before a vowel, except u, refl. pron., 2nd pers. pl.: ammo-mahuizpoloa, you dishonor yourselves; amm-altia, you bathe yourselves. Joined: 1° to the pers. pron. nech and tech, serving as object: annechmo, antechmo; annechmo-cuitlahuia, you take care of me; antechmo-cuitlahuia, you take care of us; 2° to the indef. pron. te and tla: ammote-cuitlahuia, you take care of someone or of people. If the verb is followed by a complement, te is replaced by the rel. pron. qui for the sing. and quin for the pl.: anquimo, anquimmo; anquimo-cuitlahuia in nopiltzin, you take care of my son; anquimmo-cuitlahuia in icnotlaca (Par.), you take care of the orphans; ammo-tla-cuitlahuia, you take care of something, and with the complement expressed: anquimo; anquimo-cuitia in amotlatlacol, you confess your fault, you confess your sins. RR. an, mo.
814: 2nd pers. pl. You. Joined to postpositions: you two, both together; amouan, with you; amonauac, with or near you; amoneuan, amotlan, in the midst of you, among you, with you; amopan, upon you; etc., etc.
815: poss. Your. Joined to nouns: amotlaxcal, your bread; amocolhuan, your ancestors; amoyolicatzin, an adverbial expression used in greetings: amoyolicatzin, tiacauane, ma anm-ihiyocaual-titin (Car.), good fortune, brave soldiers, may you preserve your courage. See Youic.
818: or AMUCHIUTIA, pret. OAMO-cmioti, etc.: nitla — to tin-plate an object. R. amochitl.
820: Dross, slag of tin. RR. amochitl, popoçoquillotl.
822: pret. AMOCHIUI, etc.: nitla — to tin-plate something. R. amochitl.
823: Ungrateful, one who forgets or fails to recognize a benefit. RR. a priv., mocnehilmatini.
824: Pret. OAMOLCHIUH: to make soap. RR. amolli, chiua.
825: n. v. Soap maker. R. amolchiua.
223: Or ACILLO, adj. One who has lice, full of lice. R. acelli or acilh.
224: It is not a place of rest: acemellecan aiuianyocan in tlalticpac (Car.), there is neither rest nor pleasure on earth. RR. priv. cemelle, can.
225: Particle ordinarily marking doubt and sometimes equivalent to a negation: ach aquin, I do not know who it is; ach aye (Car.), perhaps not yet; ach can, campa or canin (Car.), I do not know where; ach ça ye nelh? is it possible? is it true? ach ça ye nelli yehuatl inin yecxayaque in yectlachielice catca? is it possible that it is this person who was so beautiful, so fresh? ach iuh or iuhqui, almost so, approximately in this manner: ach iuhqui itloc quica, to resemble each other, to appear similar, speaking of two objects; ach quemman, one does not know at what time: auh quemman oc ceppa ti-Ulaquaz? ach quemman, aço tel nepantla in tonatiuh (Car.), at what time will you eat again? I do not know, no doubt at noon. See Macu.
226: Or ACHACALIN, n. Large lobster, spiny lobster, sea crayfish. RR. al, chacalin (?).
230: ACHTZA or ACHTZAN (pr. achtzan), adv. Often, frequently: in iquac itla tic-tlaçotla, achca or achtzan ompa ton-tlachia in canin ca in tlein tic-tlaçotla (Car.), when we love something, we often look toward where the thing we love is found; ça or huel achca, etc. very often; huel achtzan quimo-pohuiliaya in itlapalolocatzin tlatocacihuapilli (Car.), he recited very often the salutation to the Virgin (the Ave Maria). See Axzan.
231: CAMPA, CAN or CANIN. See Ach.
232: Pret. OACHCAUHMA: nite — to esteem, to hold someone in high regard, to place them above oneself. RR. achcauhtli, mati.
233: Pret. OACHCAUHTI: nite — to lead, to guide, to command people. R. achcauhtli.
234: Pret. OACHCAUHTITIA: to place oneself above others (aventajarse en algo). R. achcautia.
235: Or ACHCAUHTLI, n. High priest, dean of priests, chief judge, supreme judge, commissioner, chief, elder; chief, superior, or excellent thing, etc.: achcauhtli (for ti-achcauhtli), you are principal. Plur. achcacauhtin (Olm.). In comp.
236: — teachcauh or tiachcauh, elder, someone's older brother, someone's chief; uel teachcauh, the principal thing, the greatest, the most considerable; acalco teachcauh, ship's captain. R. achtli (?) or achto.
237: n. (Clav.). Military officer; plur. achcauhtin; these officers comprised the first military order, at the head of which were the Quachictin. — Name of a Chichimec chief (Clav.).
238: A little, a small amount, enough, almost: achi centlacol, a little less than half; ca achi ohui in tinech-tequiuhtia, yece ca nic-chiuaz (Car.), you are charging me with something a little difficult, but I will do it; achi muchintin, almost all; achi ye, a little more; achi ye iuhqui, a little more or a little less, approximately, about; achi ye iuhqui macuilpa, about five times; çan achi, a small amount, a little; achi yuhqui, almost so, it is similar; achi yxquich, almost as much, approximately, a little more or less, about; achi quin, I do not know, one does not know when; oc ye achi or oc achic achi, a little more, a little longer; achi miecpa in nocon-itla noteiccauh, auh in yehuatl çan quemmanian in nechhual-itia, quite often I have gone to see my brother, but as for him he rarely came to see me; achi quezquipa in ye n-alonahui, sometimes I have had a fever. Achi or oc achi, adv. of comparison: achi tiqualli in amo nehuatl, you are better than I; oc achi qualli on in amo yehuatl in, this is better than that; in mopiltzin oc achi tlatquihua in amo tehuatl, your son is richer than you. With huel, this adv. marks the highest degree of comparison: huel oc achi tiyolcocole in amo ye ce tequani (Car.), you are much more cruel than a wild beast.
240: Serving to mark the difference between persons or things: achic in tehuatl ca olite-micti, ca tic-tzaqua in motlatlacol, auh in nehuatl aic manel çan ce pinacatl onic-micti (Par.), as for you, you are a killer, you are atoning for your crime, but I have never killed even an insect; tle ipampa in amo motlatlacol? achic intla tixpopoyotl (Car.), how is it not your fault? fair enough if you were blind.
241: Of time. Recently, not long ago, soon, in a short while, etc.; oc ximo-machtitinemu, oc achic æiqu-ihiyohui, oc tipiltontli, ac quin ye ücaxtolæiuhtica, continue to study, be patient a little longer, you are still young, you have barely just turned fifteen; oc cuel achic, soon, in a moment; oc cuel achic itech nino-tzonchuiz, in a very short instant I will take my revenge on him. See Acuironca, Acmirzinca and Quix Acic.
625: pret. OALTEPETLALI: — to populate, to found a city, a town, a country. n (for #) Nin (for nino) — to revolt against the capital.
627: Founder of a city, of a state. R. altepetlalia.
628: n. v. City, action of populating a state. R. altepetlalia.
629: n. v. Founded city. R. altepetlalia.
630: Land, property of the city, generally inhabited land: anepantla ca altepetlalli ou uey — land situated in the middle of the water. RR. altepetl, tlalhi.
631: Laws, ordinances of a state. RR. altepetl, tlatlalilli.
633: Thief of public property. R. altepetlatquicaichtequi.
635: n (for ni) — to steal public property.
637: n. v. Theft of public property. R. altepetlatquicaichtequi.
639: n (for ni) — to steal public property. RR. altepetlatquitl, ichtequi.
641: n. v. Thief of public property. R. altepetlatquiichtequi.
642: Public property, property of the state. RR. altepetl, tlatquitl.
930: n. v. Negation, act of denying.
934: Ignorance, foolishness. RR. a priv. neicalilizth.
935: Joy, good humor, gaiety. RR. a priv. neyolitlacolizth.
936: adj. v. Notorious, evident, needing no explanation. RR. a priv. iximachtia.
937: n. v. Dissimulation, feigning not to see. RR. a priv. iximatcanenequi. ANEL? negative interrogative: cuix anel où amo nel ti-qu-ittaz? will you not see it? that is to say, you will see it, that is how it is; aquin yaz Quauhmanco? anel n-iaz? aquin nel yaz? who will go to Quauhmanco? shall I not go? who else will go? RR. amo, nel.
938: conj. So that; one says, they say.
940: n. of instrument. Utensil for stirring cacao. RR. atl, neloa.
941: Obscenity, turpitude, coarseness. RR. a priv. nemachilizth.
942: Negation, act of denying. RR. a priv. nemachitoquilith.
943: Turpitude, obscenity, coarseness. RR. a priv. nemalhuilizthi.
944: Audacity, effrontery, impudence. RR. a priv. nemamatiliztli.
947: Ignorance, foolishness, stupidity, clumsiness. RR. a priv. nematilizth.
745: Pret. OAMATLAPALTI : nino) — to grow wings, leaves; fig. to be
746: : nin (for rich, to have what is necessary (Olm.); m-amatlapaltia, it sprouts, puts out leaves, speaking of a tree. R. amatlapalli.
747: dim. of AMATLAPALLI. Small wing, small leaf.
750: Paper hat (capacete de papel). RR. amatl, tzoncalli.
751: A type of paper that the Indians offered to the gods. RR. amatl, tzotzomath.
752: Pret. OAMAUI : nitla — to cover, to wrap a thing in paper. R. amatl.
753: n. v. Beater of materials for paper. R. amauitequi.
754: Pret. OAMAUITEC : % (for m) — to beat, to work the materials suitable for making paper.
756: Beating of the materials used to make paper. R. amauitequi.
760: or AMAXACTLI, n. Point where a watercourse divides into several branches, fork. RR. atl, maxactli.
764: to the west of Lake Chapala (Clav.).
988: Rim of a well. RR. aolhuazth, tenth.
992: Rebellious, insubordinate, disobedient. RR. a priv. caqui.
993: Neglected, slovenly, dirty. RR. a priv. matoca.
994: or AOMMOTTA, adj. v. Neglected, dirty, slovenly. RR. a priv. itta (?).
995: adj. often accompanied by eeua. Foolish, clumsy, ignorant; aompa eeualiztli, foolishness, ignorance, clumsiness. RR. a priv. pa.
999: n. v. Rebellion, insubordination. RR. a priv. necaquilizth.
1001: n. Meanness, poverty. RR. a priv. maca.
1002: Negligence, slovenliness. RR. a priv. matoca.
1004: One who is harmful, contrary to someone. RR. a priv. aquia.
1005: and n. v. Harmful, wicked, unsociable, intractable. RR. Disgrace, rudeness, defect, coarseness. RR. a priv. nequi.
1006: n. Wickedness. RR. a priv. nequi.
1007: Wronged, defrauded. RR. a priv. aquia.
664: Mitre, paper cone. RR. amatl, copilh.
665: Pret. OAMACOPILTI: to put a paper mitre on someone. R. amacopilli.
666: to the south of Lake Chapala (Clav.).
668: Lagoon, small body of water. With the postposition «: amac, beyond the lake, the river, etc.
670: Pret. OAMAITZUN: (for ni) — to bind books. RR. amatl, tzuma. nite — to put on; nite — to dress the hair of
675: Similar to the herb amalacotl; amalacotic teocuitlatl chalchiuitl, object of gold or precious stone cut in the manner of the herb called amalacotl. R. amalacotl.
676: A kind of aquatic herb. RR. atl, malacachoa (?).
681: n. (for ni) — to sign, to seal, to affix one's signature. RR. amamachiotl, tlacuilotl, tzaqua.
682: To storm, to rain in torrents. RR. atl, mamani.
564: Without rest, without tranquility, without pleasure; ayuian nemi, he is restless, troubled, agitated.
566: A place without pleasure, without joy, without rest; ayuianyocan, acemellecan in tlalticpac (Car.), there is neither joy nor rest in this world. RR. a priv. tmianyotl, can.
573: Water level, liquid plain, horizontal surface. RR. atl, ixmanilizth.
574: With boldness, extravagance. RR. aixmauilizth, ca.
577: Bold, audacious, extravagant. RR. a priv. ixmau.
578: Or AIXNAMIQUIZTLI, n. v. Something incontestable, irresistible. RR. a priv. ixnamiqui.
580: Importunate, indefatigable; unalterable, that retains its luster, its brilliance, etc.
582: Pret. OAIXTLAUH, etc.: nino— (for nino) — to do one's hair, to arrange one's hair, speaking of a woman. RR. a priv. ixtlaua (?).
583: Not here, outside, beyond, etc.: aiz ca iyollo, he is transported, enraptured, astonished, dumbfounded; lit. his heart, his soul is not here, is beside itself; aiz uetzi noyollo, I am astonished, enraptured, transported, etc. RR. a priv. 4z.
725: : % (for ni) — to break the seal, to unseal a letter or any other written document. RR. amatlacuilolmachiotilli, ana.
727: : à (for wi) — to seal, to close a letter or a written document. RR. amatlacuilolli, machiotia.
728: One who seals or closes a letter or any written document. R. amatlacuilolmachotia.
729: n. v. Closure of a letter or any other written document. R. amatlacuilolmachiotia.
730: n. v. sealed, closed written document. R. amatlacuilolmachiotia.
732: : nite — to reply to a letter. RR. amatlacuilolli, nanquilia.
733: Place where one writes, where public scribes work. R. amatlacuiloa.
735: Note, short letter, brief written message.
736: n. v. Letter, sealed, closed written document. R. amatlacuilolizaqua.
737: pret. TZAOU : n (for ni) — to close, to seal a letter.
740: Closure of a letter. R. amatlacuiloltzaqua.
741: n. v. One who seals, who closes a letter or any other written document. R. amatlacuiloltzaqua.
742: pret. co : milla — to shelter one's chicks, to put them under one's wings, speaking of a hen. OAMATLAPALLAPA— RR. amatlapalli, lapachoa.
743: Bird's wing, sheet of paper. In the latter sense, one says especially: amaatlapalli. RR. amatl, atlapalli.
744: pret. nitla — to strip leaves. RR. amatlapalli, tepeua.
846: pret. OAMOUI: to wash oneself, to wash one's head with soap. R. amolh.
847: n. Bookshop, stationery shop. RR. amoxtli, cali.
848: Writer, scribe. RR. amoxtli, icuilou. nin (for mino) — to write oneself...
849: Pret. OAMOXITO: (for ni) — to read a book, to relate, to set forth a case. RR. amoxtli, io.
850: adj. v. Constant, stubborn, indefatigable, importunate. RR. a priv. xiuhtlatia.
851: Register, catalogue of books, title of a work. RR. amoxtli, machotl.
852: Pret. OAMOXNAMACAC: — to sell books. RR. amoxtli, namaca.
853: n. v. Bookseller, book merchant. R. amoxnamaca.
855: Bookshop, library, place where books are kept. RR. amoxtli, pia.
856: Pret. OAMOXPOUH: (for ni) — to read a book, to set forth a case. RR. n (for ni) amoxtli, poa.
857: n. v. Reader, reporter of a case, learned man, one who has knowledge and learning. R. amoxpoa.
865: Bookshop, place where books are gathered. RR. amoxtli, centeca.
826: Pret. OAMOLHUI: nn (for nino) — to soap oneself, to wash with soap. nitla — to soap something. R. amolli.
827: Pret. OAMOLLALI: nitla — to soap something, to put it in soap. RR. amolli, tlalia.
828: or AMULLI, n. Saponaria americana, a plant whose root serves as soap for the body principally (Clav.); soap in general. RR. atl, molli.
829: Pret. OAMOLNAMACAC: — to sell soap, to be a soap merchant. RR. n (for amolli), amolli, namaca.
830: n. v. Soap merchant. R. amolnamaca.
831: Pret. OAMOLON, v. n. To boil, to bubble. RR. atl, moloni.
832: n. of instrument. Utensil for stirring cacao. R. amoloni.
836: Bold, brazen, impudent. RR. a priv. momamatini.
837: Pret. OAMOMOLOGAC, v. n. To boil with large bubbles. RR. atl, momoloca.
839: or AMONAUATILMATI, adj. v. Importunate. RR. a priv. nauatilmati.
840: or AMONOTZTLANI, adj. v. Restless, sullen, taciturn, one who does not want to be spoken to. RR. a priv. notzallani.
841: One who punishes with severity, without pity. RR. a priv. pilhuia.
844: Importunate, one who does not want to leave, to be sent away. RR. a priv. totocamati.
845: One who does not want to leave, to be sent away, to be shown the door. RR. a priv. totoquilizmati.
908: Irregularly, against the law, against the rule. RR. anauatilpraliztl, ca.
909: Irregularity, violation of the law. RR. a priv. nauatilpialiath.
910: Privileged, irregular, contrary to the law, to the rule. RR. a priv. nauatilpiani.
911: (Olm.), used with the poss. a: yanca, his equal or that which is joined to another thing.
913: conj. So that, in such a way that, therefore. Used to conclude: anca amo mic-chiuaz (Olm.), so that I will not do it; anca in tlalticpac necuiltonolli netlamachtilli, anca çan nenquizcayotl anca atle itech uetziz in toyollo in iz tlalticpac, anca can yuhquin cochitleualli temictli ipan tic-matizque in tlalticpac ic pacoa (Car.), therefore the goods of the earth are but vanity, therefore we must not attach ourselves to the things of this world, therefore we must regard the pleasures of this world as dreams; anca ye huel yehua in otim-ixiti (Car.), so that you have been awake for quite some time.
914: dim. of Axqui. Somewhat long (cosa larguilla). ANE! interj. Hey! ho!
915: n. v. Dissimulation, pretense by which one makes others believe one does not understand.
917: Denial, act of denying. RR. a priv. chualyetoca.
918: pret. AONEZ, v. n. To be lacking, to not appear, to not come near: aneci notech monequi, to lack the necessities; lit. the necessary does not come to me, or does not show itself to me. RR. a priv. neci.
919: One who is lacking, who does not appear; anecini in itech monequi, poor, needy. R. aneci.
920: n. v. Ingratitude, lack of recognition. RR. a priv. necnelilmachiliztli.
921: n. v. Ingratitude. RR. a priv. necnelilmatiliztli.
924: Meanly, with miserliness. RR. anecotonalizth, ca.
927: See Avarice, meanness, poverty. RR. a priv. cotontlani.
704: n. v. Reader, one who reports on a case, judge responsible for presenting a matter. R. amapoa.
706: — (for mi) — to read everything, to read to the end. pret. OAMAPOUHCA- bout. RR. amapoa, tzonquixtia.
708: A village near the volcano of Popocatepetl (Mecameca). RR. amatl, ... (?).
710: pret. OAMATEPETI: n. (for nino) — to cover, to wrap in paper (hazer de papel algunas cosas cortadas ó labradas para ofrecer à los idolos).
712: To the N.W. of Lake Chapala (Clav.).
713: n. Paper, card, etc.: uei apan amatl, nautical chart. In comp. namauh (for no-amauh), my paper; amauh (for in-amauh), their paper. With the postposition c: amac (Par.), in the paper.
714: n. v. Writer: uei amatlacuilo, secretary, head writer; pl. amatlacuiloque; in tiachcauh in amatlacuiloque, chief writer, the first, the head of the writers. R. amatlacuiloa.
715: pret. OAMATLACUILO: n. (for ni) — to write a letter. RR. amatl, cuiloa.
718: pret. OAMATLACUILOLCUEP: nite — to write to someone, to reply to a letter. v. Place where one writes.
720: n. Postage. RR. amatlacuilolli, tiqui, daxtlauilli.
721: Office of public writer. R. amatlacuilon.
722: n. Acknowledgment, written attestation. RR. amatlacuilolli, tlaneltilizth.
723: n. v. Letter, epistle. R. amatlacuiloa.
1030: Pret. APACHIUR, v. To be flooded, to fill up, to be covered with water. RR. atl, pachiui.
1032: Pret. OAPACHO: nitla — to soak, to wet something, to water plants. RR. atl, pachoa.
1033: n. Algae, seaweed, etc., any aquatic plant. RR. atl, pachth.
1034: Wet, soaked with water. RR. atl. Flood, inundation, paltic.
1035: Opening, ditch, channel for water drainage. RR. apantli, pitzactli.
1036: Pret. OAPAMPOUH: nic — to make openings for water drainage. RR. apantli, poa.
1038: To the N.E. of Tetzcuco, on the borders of the republic of Tlaxcala (Clav.).
1039: Pret. OAPAN: nin (for nino) — to dress oneself, to adorn oneself, to put on any garment. nite — to dress someone; fig. to give alms, to console (Olm.).
1040: n. Canal water. RR. apanthi, atl.
1041: Pret. OAPANAUI: nite — to take someone across a waterway or the sea, to help someone cross the water. RR. atl, panauia.
1042: That which comes from the water; wei apancayotl, something that comes, that is born in the sea, that which pertains to the sea. RR. atl, pan.
1043: Place where the Aztecs stayed for five years (Bét.).
1044: Pret. OAPANOG: n (for ni) — to cross a waterway, the sea, etc. RR. atl, pano.
1045: Pret. OAPANTECAC: n (for ni) — to build an aqueduct, a channel for water. RR. apantli, teca.
1046: Pret. OAPANTINEN: nin (for nino) — to live, to wallow, to cover oneself with something; in teuhtica, in nertica in m-apantinemi (Olm.), he lives in vice; lit. he covers himself with dust, ashes. RR. apana, nemi.
1049: Pret. OAPANTOGA: nin (for nino) — to be clothed, dressed; xolotl m-apantoc (Olm.), he is unfortunate, in misfortune and misery; lit. he is dressed like a servant. RR. apana, onoc.
1171: See Ac. ATANELLI! a kind of interjection. Too bad, so much the better.
1174: n. v. Reservoir, cistern, pond, hollow full of water. RR. atl, tataca.
1176: One who afflicts, torments others. RR. a priv. atlamachtia.
1179: n. v. Affliction, sadness. RR. a priv. atlamachtia.
1180: Or ATECAQUINI, adj. v. Arrogant, disobedient, insubmissive, rebellious. RR. a priv. caqui.
1187: Importunate, troublesome, insolent. RR. a priv. caua.
1188: Ungrateful, one who in no way values the services of a woman (Olm.). RR. a priv. teciuamatini.
866: Writer, author. RR. amox- ti, Uacuilo.
867: Bookshop, library, place where books are kept. RR. amoxtli, tlatilhia.
869: Illuminated book. RR. amoxili, Cover of book margin. dim. of tlamachia.
870: Illuminator of books. RR. amoxtli, tlamacha.
871: Illumination of books. RR. amoxtli, tlatlamachiliztli.
873: (for ni) — to authorize, confirm, legalize pret. OAMOXTLATOLNEL— a written document. RR. amoxtli, tlatolneltilia.
874: Argument, foundation of a book. RR. amoxtli, tlatolpeualizti.
875: Base, foundation, beginning, principle of a book. RR. amoxtli, tlatoltzintiliztli.
877: Division, chapter of a book. RR. amoxtli, tlatolxeloliztli.
878: Plant abundant in the lake of Mexico (Clav.); book, work; amoxtli icuitlapam mimiliuhca, the spine of a book; amoxtli itenchipauaca or iteniztaca, the margin of a book.
879: Title of a book. RR. amoxtli, tocaitl.
881: Chapter, division of a book. RR. amoxtli, xexeloliztli.
1271: and n. Ungrateful, one who is not appreciative. RR. a priv. tetlaçocamatini.
1272: With generosity, liberality. RR. atetlaçomaquihith, ca.
1273: Generosity, liberality, largesse. RR. a priv. tetlaçomaquilidthi.
1274: Malevolence, enmity. RR. a priv. tetlaçotlalizthi.
1275: Disobedient, insubordinate, rebellious. RR. a priv. tetlamacatqui.
1277: Generous, brilliant in one's expenditures, magnificent, liberal. adv. Famously.
1279: n. Generosity, liberality, munificence. RR. a priv. tamach, tlauhtia.
1280: One who punishes with rigor, without pity. RR. a priv. tetlamachuiani.
1281: Chaste, honest, modest. Said especially of a woman. RR. a priv. tlamia.
1285: Scrotum, the pouch of the testicles.
1287: Pond, water level; uei atezcatl, lake, lagoon, large pond, great water level. RR. atl, tezcatl.
1288: Tide, part of the shore covered by the tide. RR. atezcatl, tlalli, uaqui.
1289: Pret. OATEZCAUI: (for nino) — to look at oneself in the water. Nitla — to level by means of a water level. R. atezcatl.
1211: One who despises people, who is not respectful. RR. a priv. ixtilia.
1213: conj. But, and in the interrogative form, is it not evident? atel tiqu-ittaz, but you will see it; atel mochi in quimo-tzacuiltitia inic omictiloc? (Par.), is it not evident that he paid for all his faults with the death that was given to him?
1216: Effrontery, impudence, boldness. RR. a priv. mamati.
1219: One who despises or disdains others. RR. a priv. mauhqui, itta.
1222: Pret. OATEN — to be dropsical; to drown, to fill with water, speaking of a thing.
1224: Pret. OATEMI: nino — to remove one's lice, vermin. Nite atemitl. to delouse someone. R.
1225: Pret. OATEMITI: nitla — to drown, to plunge a thing into water. R. atemi.
1226: Louse. In comp.: aten (for to-aten), our lice, lice in general.
1227: adj. v. Indigestible, that does not go down.
1230: pret. OATEMOUI: v. — to brave, offend, outrage, mock someone. R. atemuitl.
1231: n. Cal., sixteenth month of the year (Clav.), regarded as «Descent, fall of water.» by some authors as the first month. Its figure represents the sign of water placed on steps, or that same sign with the sign of descending footsteps below a stone, tetl. RR. atl, temo.
1232: Covered with lice. RR. atemil, pachui.
1235: Overhang of a roof, terrace parapet. RR. atl, tenamuil.
