2132: pret. OCAMAPACHOC: nitla or nic — to say something softly, gently, to whisper; notlatol nic-camapachoa, to speak through one's teeth, to mutter; lit. I pronounce my words in the mouth, between the teeth. RR. camatl, pachoa.
2133: Cheek. In comp. nocamapan, my cheeks, my cheek; tocamapan, our cheeks, cheeks in general. RR. camatl, tepantli.
2134: pret. OCAMAPIC: — to close the mouth. RR. camatl, piqui.
2336: n. Fifteen parts, fifteen different objects, fifteen pairs; ic caxtollamantli, fifteenth part; inc caxtollamantli, the fifteenth thing or part; in caxtollamantli centlamantli, one of fifteen things; in caxtollamantli once centlamantli, one of sixteen things; in caxtollamantli omome centlamantli, one of seventeen things; in caxtollamanthi omei centlamanth, one of eighteen things; in cazætollamantli onnaui centlamantli, one of nineteen things. RR. caxtolli, tlamantli.
2337: n. used to count animate beings, thin, flat objects, etc. Fifteen; ic caxtolli, fifteenth; inic caxtolli, the fifteenth; oc caxtolh, fifteen more; caxtolli oce, sixteen; lit. fifteen one; caxtolli omome, seventeen; caxtolli omei, eighteen; caxtolli onnaui, nineteen; in caxtolli ce, one of fifteen; in caxtolli once ce, one of sixteen; in caxtolli omome ce, one of seventeen; in caxtolhi omei ce, one of eighteen; in caxtolli onnaui ce, one of nineteen. Plur. caxtolixtin; in caxtolhixtin, all fifteen. With the postpositions can, pa: caxtolcan, in fifteen parts, portions, rations, etc.; oc caxtolean, in fifteen other parts; caxtolcan oceccan, in sixteen parts; caxtolcan omoccan, in seventeen parts; caxtolcan omexcan, in eighteen parts; caxtolcan onnauhcan, in nineteen parts; caxtolpa, fifteen times; caxtolpa ixquich, fifteen times as much; oc caxtolpa, fifteen more times, fifteen other times; caxtolpa oceppa, sixteen times; caxtolpa oceppa ixquich, sixteen times as much; caxtolpa omoppa, seventeen times; caxtolpa omoppa ixquich, seventeen times as much; caxtolpa omexpa, eighteen times; caxtolpa omexpa ixquich, eighteen times as much; caxtolpa onnappa, nineteen times; caxtolpa onnappa ixquich, nineteen times as much.
2338: or GAXTULOLOTL, adj. n. Fifteen ears of corn, fifteen cacao beans, etc.; caætololotl oce, sixteen ears of corn, etc.; caxtololotl omome, seventeen ears of corn; caætololotl omei, eighteen ears of corn; caætololotl onnaui, nineteen ears of corn. RR. caxtolli, olotl.
2339: adj. n. Fifteen rows, lines, etc.; caxtolpantli oce, sixteen rows, lines; caxtolpantli omome, seventeen rows; caxtolpantlhi omei, eighteen rows; caxtolpantli onnaui, nineteen rows. RR. caxtolli, tlapantli.
2341: or CAXTULTETL, adj. n. used to count round objects. Fifteen; ic caxtoltetl, fifteenth; tic caætoltetl, the fifteenth; oc caxtolteil, fifteen more, fifteen additional; caxtoltetl oce, sixteen; lit. fifteen one; caxtoltetl omome, seventeen; caxtoltetl omei, eighteen; caxtoltetl onnaui, nineteen; in caxtolteil centetl, one of fifteen; in caxtoltetl once centeil, one of sixteen; in caxtloltetl omome centetl, one of seventeen; in caxtoltetl omei centetl, one of eighteen; in caxtoltetl onnaui centetl, one of nineteen. Plur. caxtolteixtin; in caxtolteixtin, all fifteen. RR. caxtolli, tetl.
2343: n. used to count animate beings, flat, thin objects, etc. One; ce ichth, one maguey fiber; ce icpatl, one thread of cotton or linen; ce cihuatl, one woman; ce oquichili, one man, when it is a woman speaking, and ce toquichtin, when it is a man himself; ce atlapalli, one tree leaf, plant leaf, sheet of paper, or bird's wing; ce amatlapalli, one tree or plant leaf; ce amatl cuelpachiui, one sheet of paper; ce yehuan or yehuantin, one of them (masc. or fem.); ce yehuantin in nauin tlatoque, the first of the four chiefs; ic ce, first; inic ce, the first; oc ce, another; oc ce tlacatl, another person. Plur. ceme: ceme toquichtin (Par.), one man; ceme tehuantin yaz or better tiazque (Car.), one of us will go; ceme amehuantin, one of you; ceme azcihua, æicemo-cuitlahuican in cocoxcatzintli (Car.), let one of you women take care of the sick person; ago ic nicno-yolitlacalhuiz in tlatoani, intla ceme niquin-mictili in itzcuinhuan (Car.), perhaps I would cause grief to the lord, if I killed one of his dogs. The personal pronouns ti, an combine very well with ceme: ma ticeme tehuantin tihuian (Car.), let one of us go; ma azceme amehuantin antlapixque xitla-chpanacan (Car.), let one of you, guardians, sweep. With the postpositions can, pa: ceccan or cecca, in one place, apart; ceccan mino-tlalia, to revolt; oc ceccan, in another place, in another location, another ration, etc.; ceppa, once; oc ceppa, another time; oc ceppa ic nitla-nauatia, to order something again; oc ceppa ni-yuli, to be resurrected, to come back to life, to live again; ye no ceppa, immediately a second time; ye no ceppa n-amapoa, to read another time; ceppa ce, once one. In comp. ce is written cen or cem before vowels and the consonants m, p: cencamatl, one mouthful, one word; cemaxcatl, a communal property; cemmecatl, one handful; cempantli, one row.
2344: pret. ocez, etc.: v. — to want, to consent, to agree; amo ni-cea or ani-cea, to refuse, to not want; niman amo ni-cea, to refuse entirely, to not want at all, in any way; in aquin aciaz, the one who will refuse, who will not consent. Also written ceya.
2345: Voluntarily; tecealiztica, freely, willingly, with good grace on someone's part. RR. cealizth, ca.
2346: n. v. Will. In comp. nocealiz, my will; tocealiz, our will, will in general; teceahz, will, volition, someone's resolution. With the postposition copa: nocealizcopa, of or by my will; tecealizcopa, willingly, with good grace on someone's part. R. cea.
2347: Pret. OCEALTI: nite — to persuade, to advise someone, to urge them, to lead them to want or to do something. R. cea.
2348: One who consents, who is willing. R. cea.
2350: Pret. OCECCANQUIXTI: to live apart, to keep oneself at a distance, to separate oneself from others; nino — lito-ceccanquixtia or without comp. ceccan lito-quixha, to set oneself apart, to league together, to form a coalition, or to be of different kinships. Nila — to separate one thing from another, to set it apart, etc. RR. ceccan, quixtia.
2352: Cold; atl cecec, cold water. R. cetl.
2353: (Olm.), freq. of ceacax. At each place.
2354: freq. of cecni. In various places, apart; cececni mo-tlalia, to separate; cececni quiçaliztica, piece by piece; cececni yezque in tlenamichuan (Par.), the future spouses will each be in their own home.
2355: Pret. OCEGEGNIQUIXTI: to divide a thing into several parts. RR. cececni, nitla — quixtia.
2376: Whole, intact, healthy, pleasant, pleasing. R. cecemeltia.
2377: Syncope for cecem metztica. See Metztur.
2378: Syncope for cecem metztlipan. See Metztur.
2379: Daily history, of each day, ephemeris. RR. cecemilhuitl, amoxtli.
2380: Each day, daily. RR. cecemilhuitl, ca.
2381: Used adverbially. Each day, daily. RR. cecen, ilhuitl.
2382: History of each day. RR. cecemilhuitl, tlacuiloll.
2383: Pret. OCECEMMAN, freq. of CEMMANA: nite — to depopulate a country, to disperse its inhabitants. Nitla — to spread, sow, overturn a thing, to disperse a flock; tla-cecemmana in tlatoani (Olm.), the sovereign destroys the country, governs badly.
2384: Pret. OCECEMMANCA, v. n. freq. of CEMMANI. To spread, to overturn, to disperse.
2385: Overturned, spread, sown, dispersed. R. cecemmani.
2387: Pret. OCECEMPOUH, freq. of CEMPOA: nitla — to add up, to count, to total a thing.
2388: Every twenty days. RR. cecempoalli, ilhuitl, ca.
2389: n. freq. of cempoazul. Twenty by twenty. With the postpositions can, pa: cecempoalcan, in each twenty parts; cecempoalpa, every twenty times; oc cecempoalpa, twenty more times again; oc cecempoalpa oce, twenty-one more times again; cecempoalpa ommatlatlacpa, every thirty times; lit. every twenty times and ten times; oc cecempoalpa ommatlatlacpa, thirty more times again.
2390: n. freq. of cempoaltetl. Twenty by twenty.
2391: Each, to each one; cecen tlacatl, each one, each person; cecem ilhuitl, each day. Before a c or an x the nasal is not pronounced and one writes without n: cece xihuitl, each year; cece calli, each house, or in each house; amo çan tlacecen, various and numerous things. Pl. ceceme, cecemme or cecentin. With the postposition pa: ceceppa, each time; oc ceceppa, each time again, each additional time. R. ce.
2392: Pret. OCECENCATOLHUI: nino — to play with flowers as balls.
2393: Pret. OCECENCAUH, freq. of CENCAUA: nino — to hurry, to make haste; to adorn oneself, to get ready. Nite — to arrange, adorn, dress someone. Nitla — to arrange, put in order a thing.
2356: Pret. OCECECNITLALI: to separate, to each go to a particular place, to be separated. Nitla — to put each thing apart, to separate, to distinguish. RR. cececni, tlalia.
2357: Plant whose root is used against dysentery, bile, wounds, etc. (Hern.). RR. cecec, patli.
2358: Pret. OCECEYAC: nino — to cool down; ti-ceceya, you cool down; ceceya in atl, the water cools down. R. cetl.
2359: Each one in particular, to each one; cecenyaca mo-quetza yn notzon, my hair stands on end, bristles; ceceyaca mo-quetza totzon, our hair stands on end, we are all seized with horror; ceceyaca qui-cuiz tlaxtlauil or ceceyaca totech aciz in totlaxtlauil, each one will receive his wages. R. ce.
2360: Anointed with fat, greasy, fatty; amo ceceyo, lean, having no fat. R. ceceyotl.
2361: freq. of ceyotl. Fat, tallow, bone marrow; tlaatililli ceceyotl, melted fat. In
2362: yceceyo, his fat; quaquaue yceceyo, beef fat, marrow.
2363: Pret. OCECELI: nino — to rejoice. Nite — to receive someone perfectly, to gladden them, to treat them well. RR. cen, ceha.
2364: Pret. OCECELI, freq. of CELIA: nino — to refresh oneself. Nite — to refresh someone. Nitla — to cool down hot things.
2368: Pret. OCECELIUHTOCA: welcomed, well received; in oncan ceceliuhtoc in ixquich in qualli (Olm.), where all that is good resides. RR. cecelia, onoc.
2369: Pret. OCECELMACAC: nino — to rejoice, to recreate oneself, to take pleasure. Nite — to entertain, to gladden someone. RR. cecelia, maca.
2370: Very fresh and clean, very healthy, intact. RR. cecelic, patic.
2371: Pret. OCECELTI: nino — to recreate oneself, to distract oneself, to relieve boredom. Nite — to entertain, to gladden someone. R. ceceha.
2374: Each one in particular, to each one. Plural of cecen.
2375: Pret. OCECEMELTI, freq. of CEMELTIA: nino — to rejoice, to distract oneself, to relieve boredom. Nite — to entertain, to gladden, to distract someone.
2436: Different, particular, apart. RR. cecni, quiça.
2437: Pret. OCECOCAMA: nino— to consider oneself superior to others, to hold oneself in higher esteem, to be proud; mo-cecocamati, he is presumptuous; tito-cecocamati, we agree on the same thing; ocecocamatque, they united. RR. (?), mati.
2438: Pret. OCECU: v.— to be cold, to catch cold.
2440: Cold, chilling of the body; cecuiztli nitic calaqui, to have a pain in the side; lit. the cold has entered my belly; cecuiztli tic calacqui, one who has a pain in the side; cecuiztli teitic calaquiliztli, pain in the side. R. cecui.
2441: North wind, cold wind, icy wind, biting wind, etc. RR. cetl, ehecatl.
2442: n. (Clav.). A kind of talking bird.
2444: Permission, consent. R. ceya or ciya.
2447: Alone, alone; pl. celtin; used in comp. with the possessives no, mo, i, etc.: nocel, I alone; mocel, you alone; icel, he alone; icel conetl, a single child, boy or girl; toceltin, we alone; amoceltin, you alone; inceltin or iceltin, they alone. Often this adj. is preceded by the adv. can, which serves to give more emphasis: çan nocel oni-hualla, I came all alone, just me; çan icel, one alone, by one alone. Rev. iceltzin, he alone; moceltzin or çan moceltzin, you alone; pl. amoceltzitzin, you alone. With the suffix tontli, celtontli; in comp. nocelton, I alone; toceltoton, we alone; etc.
2448: Pret. OCELCAUI: nicno— to understand a matter by oneself, to manage an affair alone.
2450: Pret. OCELIAG, OCELIZ or OCELIX (Olm.), v.n. To take, to sprout, to bud, speaking of plants; fig. to revive, to be discreet, reasonable (Olm.). Impers. tlacelia (Car.), everything turns green again.
2451: Pret. OCELI: nite— to receive, to welcome someone; to admit them, to accept them in an affair or enterprise; name-celia (for ni-amech-celia), I receive you. Nitla or nice— to receive, to take something; nic-celia teoyotica temachiotiliztli, to receive confirmation; nic-celia in sancta comunion or nic-celia in inacayotzin totecuiyo Jesu Christo, to take communion, to receive the body of Our Lord Jesus Christ; aoc nic-celia, to be full, to be unable to take any more; oqui-celi confirmacion, he is confirmed, one who has received confirmation.
2452: Fresh, green, tender, new, pleasant. R. celia.
2453: Recently, freshly, newly. See the following word. RR. celic, ca.
2454: Freshness. In comp. icelicayo or icelica in xochitl (Par.), the freshness of the flower. R. celic.
2455: Plant whose leaf resembles that of the nettle (Hern.). RR. celic, patli.
2295: Pret. OCAUHTIQUIZ: nite — to leave someone behind hastily, to depart promptly. nino — to stop along the way. RR. caua, quiça.
2296: Pret. OCAUHTIA or OCAUHTA: nino — to leave a memory of oneself, to make a will. nita or nic — to leave in memory; qui-cauhtiuh in inecauhcayo ou tetech qui-cauhtiuh in till, in tlapilli (Olm.), he departed leaving behind a good reputation through his good deeds. R. caua.
2297: Pret. OCAUI: ninolla or nicno — to reserve something for oneself in a division, to keep for oneself; to make an effort to banish shame. RR. caua.
2298: Pret. OCAUILI: nnicno or NOCOMMO — to concede, to grant something; commo-cauilia, he grants something. R. cauia.
2299: Pret. OCAUILILOTIA: ni — to inherit, to acquire by will. R. cauilia.
2300: Pret. OGAUILITIA: mite — to leave, to bequeath something to someone before departing or dying; nech-cauilitiuh, to inherit, to receive by will; lit. it is bequeathed to me. R. cauilia.
2301: Pret. OGAUINTOCA: mi — to be half dead, to be in agony. RR. çaua (?), onoc.
2303: Pret. OCAXANALTI: mitetla or mite — to make someone release a thing. R. caxani.
2304: Pret. OCAXANILPI: nita — to attach, to tie something loosely. RR. caxani, ilpia.
2305: Pret. ocaxan, v. n. To come loose, to come undone, to become untied, speaking of an object.
2306: Pret. OCAXANI: nino — to relapse, to fall ill again. nitla — to untie, to undo something, to disarm, to unstring a bow, etc. R. caxani.
2307: Pret. OGAXANILI: to lighten someone's burden or tax. R. caxania.
2309: Pret. OCAXAUAC: mi — to grow thin, to diminish. nitetla — to diminish, to decrease, to lessen, to disappear; caxaua in nococoliz, my illness is disappearing, I am convalescent. nite — to weaken someone, to relieve them. nitla — to diminish, to rough out a thing, to reduce the tribute, the tax, etc.
2310: Diminished, weakened, speaking of an ailment or illness. R. caxaua.
2311: n. v. Weakness, weakening, debility. R. caxaua.
2313: or OCAXAUHTIA: ni — to grow weak, to become weak again, to become impoverished, to lose one's wealth or see it diminish. R. caxaua.
2236: Pret. OCAQUILI: nitetla or nicte — to listen, to hear what someone says, to grant someone's request. R. caqui.
2237: Pret. OCAQUILTI, etc.: nitella or nicte — to notify someone of something; to make a deaf person understand something; to invite to a meal; to mock, to joke, to criticize. Rev. of caqui.
2238: Pret. OCAQUIZTIC: timbre of voice; to resonate, to ring, to be clear, to make oneself well understood; wel ni-caquizti or niuel-caquizti, to resonate well; aniuel-caquizti, to resonate poorly; nouiyan or ipa-nocni-caquizti, to resonate, to reverberate on all sides. R. caqui.
2239: Clarity, sonority. In comp. icaquiztica, with or for its clarity; ou in icaquiztica, difficult to understand; amo oui in icaquiztica, easy to grasp; lit. not difficult for its clarity. R. caquizti.
2240: Pret. OCAQUIZTILI: nitla — to declare, to attest, to comment, to interpret, to annotate, to expound, to explain something. R. caquizti.
2242: Sonorous, producing a pleasant sound. R. caquizti.
2243: Sound; a person of credit; wel caquizti, pure, clear, true sound. R. caquizti.
2245: Place, location where one stands or stays. In comp. nocatcayan, in my place (en mi lugar ó assiento). See Catcayan. RR. catqui, yan.
2246: Then, finally, definitively, lastly; achtopa æim-ixami, çatepan ti-teniçaz (Gar.), wash your face first, then you will have breakfast.
2247: Place, seat. In comp. nocatyan, my place, my seat, my dais. RR. ca, yan.
2248: Or CATTICA, pret. OCATICATCA, etc.: to be, to be found somewhere; onnech-catica, it is far from me, there is a distance from here to there. RR. ca, ca.
2249: Pret. OCATICACA or OCATICAYA: ni — to be standing, to stand upright; teixpan ni-caticac, to be in someone's presence. RR. ca, icac.
2251: CATLEIN or CATLI? interrog. pron. Which? who? ticatli? have you not yet returned? (aun no has buelto?) catli in Pedro? (Olm.), where is Pedro? catli ye? or catleuatl? which of them? Plur. catleque, catleique, catleime? (Olm.). Without interrog.: qui-matiz catlehuatl in qualli, yectli qui-chihuaz (J. B.), he will know what it is fitting for him to do. RR. can, tle.
2252: Irregular, particular form of the verb ca, to be, to be found: sing. ni-catqui, ti-catqui, catqui; plur. ti-catqui, an-catqui, catqui. Used almost exclusively in the 3rd person singular of the present indicative: wel ompa catqui, he is over there; catqui in amatl (Olm.), there is paper; a catqui ic nic-melahua (J.B.), this is how I understand it; in catqui in onopan mo-chiuh (Car.), this is what happened to me.
2253: Pret. OCATZACTIAC or OCATZACTIX: ni — to become blackened, to become dirty. R. catzaua.
2036: Portal, frontispiece, facade of a house. RR. calli, irquaitl or ixquatl.
2037: Vestibule, door. With the postpositions copa, pan: calixcopa, on the front of the house or the facade itself; calixpan, in front of the house; tecalixpan, in front of someone's house, before his door. RR. calli, ixtli.
2038: Portal, facade of a house. RR. calli, ixæotl.
2039: Dwelling, hamlet, group of houses; calla pouhqui, common, which is said or occurs among common people. RR. calli, tla.
2040: Pret. OCALLALI: nite — to shut someone in at home, in their dwelling; to dismiss a servant. RR. calli, tlalia.
2041: Land, field surrounding a house, the very site itself. RR. calli, tlalli.
2043: Pret. OCALLANEUHTI: nite — to rent a house to someone. RR. calli, tlaneuhtia.
2044: Pret. OCALLANEUI: nino — to rent a house, to pay the rent for oneself; techan mo-callaneuia, he lives in someone else's house. RR. calli, tlaneua.
2048: House, dwelling, hut, box; wooden or reed tongs for eating corn cooked on ashes; raven; we calli, dirty; vino calli, cellar, wine-store. Calli, name of a year and the third day of the month; 1 calli xihuitl, 1325, ypan inin acico inic motlalico atlitic Tenuchtitlan in Mexica Chichimeca (Chim.), year two house, then the Mexica Chichimecs came to settle in the lagoon at Tenochtitlan. In comp. nocal, my house; ce nocal oxixin (Par.), a house of mine collapsed; mocal, your house; cal, his house; tncal in nocolhuan (Par.), the house of my ancestors. With the postpositions itic, iticpa, nauac, pan, tech, tlan, tlampa, tzalan: calitic, inside the house, in the interior; mocalitic, inside your house; calitic mononequi, domestic provisions, everything necessary in houses; calitic mitla-uelmana, to tile the interior of a house; caliticpa or calitecpa, inside the house, from the interior; calitecpa calixtli, interior door; caliticpa non-tlachia, to look inside a house; calnauac, near the house; nocalnauac, near my house; nocalnauac tlacatl, my neighbor; lit. person near my house; calpan, in the house, at home; cecen calpan, in each house, at each house; caltech or caltitech, against the house; caltitech xic-quetza in quauitl (Olm.), lean the wood against the house; callan or callampa, outside the house, beside the house; caltitlan, near the house; nocaltitlan (Par.), near my house; caltzalan, between the houses. R. ca.
2049: Pret. OCALLOTI: nite — to lodge someone; tepal nino-callotia, to live at others' homes; nino — to lodge oneself; techan mo-callotia, he is housed in someone's home. Nite — to lodge someone, to give them hospitality, to receive them in one's house; to spy on someone, to watch, to examine where they enter. Nitla — to make a setting to mount an object. R. calli.
2050: (?), n. Box, case, sheath. In comp. nocallo, my case; icallo, his case; tepuztepatiloni icallo, case for surgical instruments. R. calli.
2051: Pret. OCALMACAC: nite — to give a house to someone. RR. calli, maca.
2052: Pret. OCALMAN: nitla — to build, to erect houses. RR. calli, mana.
2054: Architect, builder of houses. R. calmana.
2618: Much, very, quite; çan cenca, invariable, stable, immutable; cenca iztac, very white; cenca mauzlie, wonderful; cenca miec, cenca yxachi or cenca uey, much, extremely; cenca moce-celtiani, utterly debauched, depraved; cenca nelhi, true, positive; oc cenca, oc cenca ye or yehuatl, or simply cenca yehuatl, above all, principally, especially: oc cenca ye nic-nequi or niqu-eleuia, I want this principally, I prefer it; used in comparatives and superlatives: oc cenca ye a, much more (See YE); oc cenca amo, less; oc cenca mec in marca, in mollatqui in amo nehuatl (Cax.), you have more goods than I; cenca aqualh, worse, more badly, more evil; cenca aquallotl, worsening, perversity, detriment; ça cenca huel mahuizticatzintl (Car.), very honorable. RR. cen, ca.
2619: A family; lit. a complete house. Pl. cencaltin, the people of a household; cencalthcate, we are all together in one house. RR. ce, call.
2620: The action of opening the mouth, a yawn. RR. ce, camachalohzth.
2621: A mouthful, a word; cencamail ic nic-cuepa im motlatoltzin, I reply a word to your speech or discourse. RR. ce, camail.
2622: Pret. OCENGAC: nino — to amend oneself, to listen, to follow counsel faithfully. RR. cen, caqui.
2623: Pret. OCENCAUH: nino — to adorn oneself, to dress up, to arrange oneself, to present oneself well; an ülhuitzin uel ipan timo-cencauaz (Olm.), you will adorn yourself fittingly on feast days. Nite — to adorn, arrange, ornament someone. Nitla — to ornament, arrange, put in order, season, prepare a thing. RR. cen, caua.
2624: Pret. OCENGAUALTI: mitetla — to take from someone everything they have, to disinherit someone.
2626: Pret. OGENGAUALTITOCA: everything is ready, it is just right. RR. cencaualtia, onoc.
2627: used in comp.: in yoltexth, flour blossom. R. cencaua.
2628: Pret. OCENGAUILI: mitetla — to prepare, arrange a thing for others, to put in order, to — icencauhca restore public affairs. R. cencaua.
2629: Pret. OCENCHIUH: mo — to be done in a single stroke, all at once, speaking of a thing. RR. cen, chiua.
2630: A unit of length, a fathom, from the armpit to the hand. RR. ce, ciacatl.
2631: A large serpent with varied colors that shines in the dark (O. y B.). RR. centh (?), coatl.
2632: True darnel, a plant that resembles the stalk of maize. With the postp. tlan: cencocopi-üitlan, among the darnel, in the midst of the darnel. RR. centhi, copina (?).
2633: A place full of darnel. RR. cencocopi, tla.
2635: A width of cloth, a piece of fabric; ce cençotl icopo, a piece of cloth matching another, to be sewn together. See Cocoxoria. RR. cen, çotl.
2636: A plot of ground between two furrows; a squadron of soldiers. RR. ce, cuemuitl.
2637: Pret. OCENGUIG: nino — to continue, to finish a thing; to take everything there is. RR. cen, cui.
2718: pret. OCENTETTIA, v. n. To be in agreement, to be in tune, speaking of singing. R. centetia.
2719: pret. OCENTETTIUITZA, v. n. To go together, to form a whole; acallhi centettiuitz, a fleet, a gathering of ships. RR. centetia, uitz.
2720: A batch of bread or of another thing. RR. ce, texcall.
2723: Who are of the same age, speaking of two persons. RR. cen, uitz.
2724: (Clav.) Village of Tabasco, near which the soldiers of Cortés won their first victory (25 March 1519). — District of Tlatelolco (Bét.).
2725: A cluster (of grapes, dates, etc.). RR. ce, tlaanthi.
2726: pl. Persons of the same family; ticentlaca, we are all of the same family, of the same nation. RR. cen, tlacatl.
2727: pret. OCENTLAZ: to reject, to repel someone or something; mc-centlaça noyollo or in noyollo, to lose, to alienate someone's friendship; to resolve to reject something, to correct oneself, to amend oneself. RR. cen, tlaca.
2730: or CENTLACHIPINTLI, n. A drop of liquid. RR. ce, chipini.
2732: dim. of the preceding. A small droplet.
2733: Half, fraction, portion of a thing; centlacol icxitl, a half-foot; centlacol metztli, half-moon; centlacol quamatlatl, half-net; centlacol quauhacaltonthi, a measure, the twelfth part of a fanega; centlacollatamachiualon, half a pound. RR. ce, tlacoa.
2734: pret. OCENTLACOLHUI: nite — to share a thing equally with someone. R. centlacoloa.
2735: pret. OCENTLACOLO: nitla — to divide, to cut a thing in half. R. centlacol.
2737: A stitch (lit. a needle point). RR. ce, tlaçotl.
2537: A unit of length, one fingernail. RR. ce, iztetl.
2538: pret. OCEMMACAC: ninole — to submit oneself to someone, to devote oneself, to give oneself entirely to them.
2540: A spindle, a ball of thread. RR. cemmalacatl, tenth.
2542: pret. OCEMMAMA: nic — to rule, to govern everyone. Nitla or nic — to atone for the faults of others. RR. cen, mama.
2543: pret. OCEMMAMALTI: nieno — to burden one's conscience. RR. cemmama.
2544: pret. OCEMMAN: mo — to spread continuously; mo-cemmana in quiauitl, it rains incessantly. Nite — to drive away, to put people to flight, a herd, to disperse the enemy. Nitla or nic — to divulge, to publish news, to chase, to disperse a herd, to spread, to send away, to throw to the ground, to bring together, etc.; nic-cemmana in tlatolli, to distinguish oneself, to acquire renown. RR. cen, mana.
2545: Perpetually, always, sincerely; cemmanca yeni, a stable and perpetual thing; cemmanca nemiizthi, eternal life; cemmanca necuiltonoliztli, imperishable, eternal good. R. cemmant.
2546: pret. OCEMMANCA, v. n. To disperse, to spread, to separate, to go apart; to be simple, smooth, flat, even, speaking of an object. RR. cen, mani.
2547: Entirely, formally, finally, perpetually; cemmanyan nino tlatzon-tequilia or nino-tlatlalilia, to make a firm resolution to amend oneself; intla ye oti-mic, ca ye ic cemmanyan t-ilcauhtoz (Par.), once dead, you will be forgotten forever. RR. cen, mani.
2549: adj. v. Equal, flat, smooth, dispersed. R. cemmani.
2550: To go together, to form a whole; acalli cemmantiuitz, a fleet, ships that are gathered together. RR. cemmam, uitz.
2551: A handful, all that can be contained in the hand.
2553: dim. of CEMMAPICHTLI. A small handful.
2555: A unit of length, one finger.
2557: pret. OGEMMA: mino — to be proud, arrogant; mo-cemmati, he is presumptuous, vain. Mitla — to be very devoted to a matter, to reflect deeply, to give oneself exclusively to one thing. RR. cen, mat.
2558: A unit of length, one fathom (brasse). In comp. nocemma or nocemmauh (Olm.), my fathom. See Ceuwire. RR. ce, maül.
2559: Pret. OCEMMATTIA: nic — to pursue, to finish what has been started. R. cemmati.
2561: A unit of length, one cubit. RR. ce, matzotzopazllhi.
2562: Pret. OCEMMAUIZCUIC: nit — to apprehend, to dread something, to be terrified, frightened, to always be afraid. RR. cen, mauizcur. CEMMECATL, n. A handful of small things, a bundle, bunch of garlic, onions, or chilli; a plot of land. RR. ce, mecatl.
2563: dim. of the preceding. A small handful, a small bundle.
2564: pret. OCEMMELAUAC: mic — to explain something obscure and difficult. RR. cen, melaua.
2566: etc.: mitla — to surpass other persons or things. Sometimes the verb ca is joined to this verb: cemmiauayotitca. RR. cen, miauayotia.
2567: n. v. One who dies forever. RR. cen, miquini.
2568: A unit of length, one cubit (medida desde un codo hasta la otra mano). RR. cen, mantl.
2569: Or CEMIXCUEPA, pret. OCEMMIX— ougp, etc.: nit — to lose one's way, to get lost, to be unable to find one's path again. RR. cen, ixcuepa.
2572: A unit of length, one cubit. RR. ce, molicpitl.
2573: A bunch of grapes, a branch of palm or plane tree.
2574: Pret. OCEMOLOLO: nitla — to atone for the faults of others, to pay for everything that others have done or committed. RR. cen, ololoa.
2575: dim. of cemolotl. A small ear of maize.
2576: n. An ear of maize or a similar thing. RR. ce, olotl.
2598: n. Four hundred (garments, bundles, loads of clothing); lit. twenty twenties. RR. cempoalli, quimilh.
2599: n. used for counting round objects. Twenty; followed by the adj. n. oce, omome, omei, onnaui, ommacuilli, onchiquace, onchicome, onchicuei, onchicunaui, ommailactli, ommatlactli oce, ommatlactl omome, ommatlactli omei, ommatlactli onnaui, oncaxtolli, oncaxtolli oce, oncaxtolli omome, oncaxtolli omei, oncaxtolli onnaui, this adjective serves to designate the numbers from twenty-one to thirty-nine; ic cempoaltetl, twentieth; inic cempoaltetl, the twentieth; in cempoaltell centeil, twenty-one; ên cempoaltell ontetl, twenty-two; etc. RR. cempoall, tetl.
2600: Fairs, markets that recur every twenty days. RR. cempoalli, tianquiztli.
2601: adj. n. used for counting animate beings, etc.; 7th order of units. Sixty-four million. RR. cempoalli, tzontli, xiquipilli.
2602: adj. n. used for counting animate beings, flat objects, etc.; 5th order of units. One hundred sixty thousand. RR. cempoalli, xiquipilli.
2603: Period of twenty years, spanning twenty years. R. cempoalxiuitl.
2606: Caryophyllus mexicanus (Hern.). A very common plant in Mexico, acclimatized in Europe and commonly called the marigold (Clav.). RR. cempoalli, xochatl.
2607: Pret. OCEMPOCTLANTIC: to lose, to destroy oneself entirely. RR. cen, poctlanti.
2608: He or that which perishes forever, which disappears entirely. R. cempohui.
2609: Pret. OCEMPOLIUH: to perish, to disappear entirely, forever. RR. cen, poli.
2612: Pret. OCEMPOPOLHUI: to destroy a thing entirely, to pardon someone for all their offenses. Mitetla — to completely annihilate someone's possessions. RR. cen, popolhuia.
2613: Pret. OCEMPOPOLO: nitla — to destroy a thing entirely, leaving nothing of it remaining; nic — to de-
2615: Pret. OCEMPOUHTITLAZ: nic — to utterly despise, to completely disdain someone. RR. nite or cempoa, tlaça.
2616: Entirely, completely, jointly; uel cen, completely, entirely; cen xihuitl, the whole year; çan cen, together, in one go, in a single place; çan cen tlacalque, equal in age, born at the same time; çan cen tlacatitilmath, a garment without a seam; çan cen nic-caqui, to understand, to grasp from the first time. In comp. and before vowels or the letters m and p, one writes cem: nite-cemicxotla, to utterly despise someone; nitetla-cemmaca (Olm.), to give a thing entirely to someone; ni-cempoliui, to perish, to die forever; etc. R. ce.
2517: Pret. OCEMITALHUI: nitla or nicno — to propose something boldly, resolutely, to decide, to take a position, a firm determination; oquimo-cemitalhui in inenca (Olm.), he (God) has fixed the nourishment of each one. RR. cemitta, ilhuia.
2518: plur. A family; lit. those of the same house. RR. ce, icalli.
2519: plur. Uterine children, born of the same womb. RR. ce, ititl.
2520: Pret. OCEMITO: nitla or nic — to deliberate, to propose boldly, with confidence, to express the resolution to do something; tic-cemitoa, we are largely of the same opinion, we all have the same resolution. RR. cen, itoa.
2521: Resolution, plan, exposition of some matter, of a case; used in comp. and often accompanied by centeneualoca: icemitoloca icenteneualoca, his resolution. R. cemitoa.
2522: Pret. OCEMITQUIC: nic — to rule, to govern everyone. RR. cen, itqui.
2523: Pret. OCEMITTAC: nitla — to look at, to examine someone from all sides. Nitla attentive, very focused on something. RR. cen, itta.
2524: Pret. OCEMIXCAC: nic — to forewarn someone, to alert them about something; to be very —
2525: Pret. OCEMIXCAUI: nic — to be occupied with a single thing, to be skilled only in that. RR. cen, ixcauia.
2529: Flat surface (of plants), state of that which is completely smooth. RR. cen, ixmaniliztli.
2530: Flat, smooth and even like a table or the ground. RR. cen, ixmanqui.
2531: Pret. OCEMIXNAHUATI: nino — to propose something resolutely, to amend oneself, to correct oneself, to make a resolution, to promise to do; ¿cux huel timo-cemixnahuatia inic aocmo ceppa ti-tlatlacoz? (J. B.), do you firmly resolve to sin no more? Nite — to dismiss, to send someone away for good; to judge, to condemn someone.
2533: Pret. OCEMIXNAHUATITIA: nite — to leave someone, to depart forever, to go away never to return. R. cemixnahuatia.
2534: Pret. OCEMIXTI: nitla — to join, to assemble flat things. RR. cen, ixtia.
2535: A load of wood. RR. ce, ixtlapalli.
2536: Used in the expression: cemixtli uic, all eyes are turned, fixed on the same point. RR. cen, ixtli.
2758: A mouthful (a gulp). RR. ce, tlaltequilizth.
2759: dim. of CENTLAL-TEQUILIZTH. A small mouthful.
2760: pret. OCENTLAMACHTI: to glorify, to enrich someone. nite- or nic- — to enjoy something. RR. cen, machtia.
2763: n. One, a (part, pair, or thing different from another); centlamantli tonacayo, a limb, a part of the body; centlamantli meneuhque, part of two equal things; oc centlamantli, something else, in another manner; oc no centlamantli, yet another thing; oc cenca centlamantli, very different, quite another thing. Plur. centlamantin, a great number, a herd, a team, etc.: centlamantin yaoquizque, a squadron of soldiers; centlamantin ichcame, a flock of sheep; centlamantin quaquaueque, a herd of oxen or cows; centlamantin oztomecacauallome, a troop of pack animals. RR. ce, tlamantli.
2764: chickpeas, or similar things). RR. ce, tlamapictli.
2765: Or CENTLAMATZOLOLLI, n. v. A handful (of grains, etc.). RR. ce, matzoloa. A handful (of peas, etc.).
2766: pret. OCENTLAMI: nitla- or nic- — to eat something entirely, to finish it, to leave nothing of it; to venture, to risk something. RR. cen, tlamia.
2768: : nite- or nic- — to take leave of someone in order to go far away or to a foreign land. R. centlamia.
2769: A throw of a stone. RR. ce, tlamotlalli.
2770: A handful, a type of measure. RR. ce, tlamotzolli.
2771: n. v. A fathom of thickness; centlanauatectli quauitl ou tlaquetzalli, a tree or column one fathom in thickness. RR. ce, nauatequi.
2772: In the abyss, at the deepest point, in the open sea; centlani mictlan quinmotlaxilia in totecuiyo in tlatlacoanime (Car.), Our Lord casts sinners to the depths of hell. RR. cen, tlan.
2774: A mass (of fat or similar substance). RR. ce, tlaolololli.
2775: On one side; oc centlapal, on the other side; ca ye otlehuac iz centlapal noyomotlan, xitlanahuati ma nech-cuepacan inic ni-tlehuaquiz in oc centlapal (Car.), already one of my sides is burned, command that they turn me over so that I may burn on the other side.
2776: n. v. An ear of wheat or of another grain. RR. ce, quechcui.
2777: A handful of maguey or of flax. RR. ce, quetza.
2698: n. Twenty (houses, stones, and all inanimate things); lit. a row, a group. Plur. centecpantin (for animate beings), twenty persons, twenty head of cattle, etc. RR. ce, tecpana.
2699: pret. OCENTECPANUI: nite — to have frequent relations with a woman, to possess her often. RR. centeca, panuia.
2700: plur. (Clav.). Commissioners charged with supervising and informing the magistrates. RR. centeca, tlapixqui.
2702: A squadron, a platoon of soldiers. RR. ce, tecuitli.
2703: pret. OCENTELCHIUH: nite — to despise, disdain, completely reject someone. nitla — to reject a thing, to disown. RR. cen, telchiua.
2704: adj. v. Despicable; in tlallacolli ca centelchiualoni (Par.), sin is execrable, must be detested. R. centelchiua.
2705: pret. OCENTEN — nitla — to pile up, to heap things somewhere. RR. cen, tema.
2706: A sowing, a planting. RR. centetl, milh.
2707: A squadron of soldiers. RR. ce, tenamuil.
2708: used in comp. — icenteneualoca, his plan, his resolution. RR. cen, teneua.
2709: A word; mc-cuepa in mihiyoLin, in motlatoltzin ycententh ycencamatl in popoloni (Olm.), I answer your speech with the word of a mute. RR. ce, tentli.
2710: (Clav.). Goddess of the earth and of maize, worshipped principally among the Totonacs. According to the degree of ripeness of the maize, she was also called Tainteotl, Xilonen, Tztacacenteotl, and Tlatlauhqui Centeotl, or else Tonacayohua, she who nourishes us; etc. The Mexicans had dedicated five temples to her and celebrated three feasts in her honor: the first, at the beginning of the month hueitozoztlhi; the second, at the beginning of the month hueitecuilhuitl; the third, at the end of the month ochpanizli. She was also the goddess of lapidaries, who celebrated her feast around the middle of the month teotleco. RR. centli, teotl.
2711: pret. OCENTEQUILI: ticto — to all be of the same opinion, to share the same point of view. RR. cen, tequilia.
2712: pret. OCENTETIAC, OCENTETIX or OCENTET, v. n. To be united, to agree, to be as one; centetia in toyollo, we love one another, our hearts are united; centetia in intlatol, they are of the same opinion; amo centetia in totlatol, we do not agree, our way of thinking is not the same; çan acentetia itlatol, he speaks falsely; çan acentetia tetlatol, it is deceit, duplicity of speech; lit. the speech of people is not the same, not one, etc. R. centetl.
2713: pret. OCENTETILI: mo — to come together, to form a whole, to make a unit, to gather into a ball, a cluster, etc. nitla — to join various things and make only one of them. R. centetia.
2714: pret. OCENTETITIA, v. n. To go along in tune, with harmony, with ensemble, speaking of singing. R. centetia.
2715: n. used for counting round objects, such as stones, fruits, eggs, hills, etc. One; centetl capullotl, one grain (of grape, etc.); centetl ichcatomitl, one fleece, wool; centetl yachcho or ixinachcho, one seed grain; centetl quaquaue, one head of large cattle; centetl quaquauhtentzone, one head of small cattle; oc centetl, another, one other. RR. ce, tetl.
2716: Whole, entire, that which is one.
2778: A slice, a round slice, a piece; centlatectli tocino, a ham, a piece, a slice of pork. RR. ce, tequi.
2781: n. A heap of small things, such as wheat, corn, etc. RR. cen, tlatepeuhtitlalilli.
2782: freq. of cenrcarecru. A skein of thread, silk, etc.; lit. that which is unwound, wound into a ball.
2783: A piece, a fragment of any kind. RR. ce, tlatlapantli.
2784: Common good. In comp. lecentlatqui, public domain, that which belongs to everyone.
2786: n. A part, a piece, a fragment. RR. ce, tzayana.
2787: A heap (of corn or small things). RR. cen, tzoneua, tlalia.
2790: pret. OCENTLATZONTEQUILI: nite — to judge, to condemn persons at the same time or forever. RR. cen, tzontequiha.
2791: A bunch of garlic, a string or bundle of onions, etc., everything that is in order, that is arranged. RR. ce, uipana.
2792: n. A distaffful; centlaxochtli ychcatl, a distaff-load of cotton or wool. RR. ce, tlaxochth.
2793: A slice of something. RR. ce, tlaxotlalh.
2794: With a slice, a piece. RR. centlaxotlalli, ca.
2795: n. Stalk, dry ear of corn; centli yyzuayo, leaf of a dry ear of corn.
2796: pret. OCENTOCAC: nitla — to buy everything, to leave nothing. RR. cen, toca.
2797: All summer, or the dry season; centonal cana ni-nemi, I have here the heat, the summer, a dry season. RR. cen, tonallh.
2898: Annals, history by years. RR. cexiuul, tlacuilolh.
2900: Pret. OCHAGAYOLIUH. v. n. To have calluses, corns.
2901: n. Corns, calluses that form on the feet and hands (callos que se les hazen á las mugeres encima de los pies de estar moliendo en metlatl). Annual tribute.
2902: Lobster, crayfish, freshwater shrimp from the lake of Mexico (Hern.).
2903: One who has calluses. R. chachacayoliur.
2904: Pret. OCHACHACAYOLIUH, freq. of cmacayoziui. To have many calluses; chachacayoliui in noma, my hands are full of calluses.
2906: Pret. OGHAGHACHALACAC, freq. of cHaLANI: ni — to cry out, to speak loudly.
2907: Pret. OGHAGHAYAGAG, freq. of cHAYAUr. To spread on the ground, to fall little by little, to fade, to disappear, speaking of things.
2908: Pret. OCHACHAYACATIMANCA, v. n. To be sparse, scattered, etc. RR. chachayaca, mani.
2909: Pret. OCHACHAYACATOCA, v. n. To be sparse, speaking of houses, trees, etc. (Olm.). RR. chachayaca, onoc.
2910: Pret. OCHACHAYATZ, freq. of cHAYAUI: nitla — to scatter something on the ground such as wheat, maize, etc.
2911: Pret. OCHAGHAYAUR; freq. of cHAYAUA: nitla — to spread something on the ground (wheat, maize, etc.).
2913: Pret. OCHACHAYAUH, freq. of cHAYAUr. To spread out, to disperse, speaking of an object.
2914: Pret. OGHAGHALACAC, freq. of CHALANI: ni — to talk a great deal, to chatter, to make noise; chachalaca, it sings, one who sings; a bird, a type of pheasant whose song is continuous (Clav.).
2915: n. v. Murmur, chatter, babbling, noise of vessels being broken. See CnacnaLaquizizTLir. R. chachalaca.
2916: A tree that bears berries; — a noisy bird (Hern.).
2917: n. v. Talker, chatterbox. R. chachalaca.
2660: OR cennecxiantli, n. A step, a stride, a great step (passada tendida). RR. ce, necxianalizthi.
2661: In the blink of an eye. RR. cenneixeueyoniliztli, ca.
2662: A blink of the eyes, that is to say a brief instant; one also says: auel cenneixcueyonilizth, a very brief instant, not even enough time to open and close one's eye. RR. ce, iLCUeyonia.
2663: pret. OCENNELO: to mix several things together. RR. cen, neloa.
2664: Pret. OCENNEN: nitla — to mix; to exist, to last all one's days; ti-cennemi, to live together, to cohabit (en uno vivir dos). RR. cen, nemi.
2665: Life of those who dwell and live together; eternal life. R. cennemi.
2666: Pret. OCENNEPANO: to stir several things together. RR. cen, nepanoa.
2667: A unit of length, the height of a man. RR. ce, quetza.
2668: Or cennetlalolli, n. v. A run, a league. RR. ce, tlaloa.
2669: A racecourse, track, place where one practices running. RR. ce, tlaloa.
2672: A course or row (of stones, bricks, etc.). RR. ce, tepantli.
2673: Pret. OCENQUALTILI: nite — to make someone perfectly good. RR. cen, qualtilia.
2674: A unit of capacity, one fanega. RR. ce, quauhacalli.
2675: A unit of capacity, one spoonful. RR. ce, quauhcuematli.
2640: n. (Olm.). Master, possessor of corn stalks. R. centh.
2642: pret. OCENYAGAN: to command, govern, rule a country. RR. cen, yacana.
2644: pret. OCENIA: ti — to go, depart, march together; to be in agreement, in tune, when speaking of singing; ti-cenui, we go somewhere together, we fall at the same time; ocenyaque, they all departed; ti-cenyazque, we will all go together. RR. cen, yauh.
2645: Family, household, people living together. In comp. icenyeliz, his family; yaoquizque incenyeliz, camp, gathering of troops. RR. cen, yelizth.
2646: Or CENYOUAL, adv. At night, throughout the whole night; cenyoal itztoni, ixtlatuini or ixtoçoani, night watchman, one who stays up all night; cenyohual onin-aman, cenyohual onino-cuitlacueptinen (Car.), all night I was restless, I did nothing but toss and turn. R. cenyoalh.
2647: Or CENYOUALLI, n. One night. RR. ce, yoalli.
2648: pret. OCENYOALTI: ni — to last all night, to spend the night somewhere. R. cenyoal.
2649: Cordiality. In comp.: mocenyollo, your cordiality. With the postpositions ca, copa: tecenyolloca or tecenyollocopa, affectionately, cordially, intimately, wholeheartedly; nocenyollocopa or nocenyollocacopa, with all my heart. RR. cen, yollotl.
2650: A unit of length measurement, one fathom (desde el pecho à la mano). RR. cen, yolloth.
2651: Everywhere, in all places. RR. ceh, ipanocan.
2654: n. A quarter of something, a slice, a piece (of meat, of bread, etc.). RR. ce, nacazth.
2655: pret. OGENNAUATI: nino — to resolutely determine oneself to something, to make a firm resolution. See Cemwixnauatia. RR. cen, nauatia.
2656: pret. OCENNAUATITIA: nite — to take leave of someone in order to depart and go to a foreign land, with the sense of not returning. R. cennauatia.
2878: Frozen, dying of cold. R. ceuechihlo.
2879: Full power, omnipotence. RR. cen, uehtilizth.
2880: Pret. OCEUETZ, Pass. ceuechililo. RR. cetl, ueta.
2881: n. v. Place where things stop, come to rest. In comp. in iceuhyan in atl (Par.), place where waters gather. — v. To freeze, to be cold. RR. cew, yan.
2882: Calmed, tamed, appeased, fallen, having declined in glory or dignity. R. ceui.
2883: Pret. oceuuia: — to go into decline, to fall. R. ceur.
2884: Pret. OCEUR: — to calm down, to spread, to cool off; tletl ceui, the fire, the lamp goes out. Pass. ceuilo, to be frozen, speaking of plants. R. cetl.
2885: Pret. OCEUI: itechpa in notequiuh, to suspend one's work, one's duties. nino — to rest; nino-ceuia tions. nite — to relieve someone, to help them carry a burden, to console, to show mercy; to restrain, to calm an irritated person. nita — to cool something, to extinguish (the fire, the lamp, etc.). Rev. ceuitzinoa. R. ceui.
2886: Pret. ocEuILI: nitella — to extinguish someone's fire or lamp; to appease, to reconcile people who are enemies. R. ceuia.
2887: v. Taming, the act of making calm, gentle, etc. R. cewilia.
2889: Frozen, dying of cold, speaking of persons and animals. R. cewilo.
2891: n. History of one year, annals.
2893: Pret. OCEXIUHTI: — to be one year old, to be or remain one year in a place; n-cexiuhtiz (Olm.), I will stay there one year; ye cexiuhtia coyametl, a one-year-old piglet; ye cexiuhtia icheatl, a lamb that is one year old. R. cexiuitl.
2894: During one year, at the end of one year. RR. cexiuitl, ca.
2895: Pret. OCEXIUHTILI: — to remain one year somewhere; ni-cexiuhtiliz (Olm.), I will stay there one year. R. cexiuhtia.
2678: Pret. OCENQUETZ: nitla — to persevere in an undertaking, to deal at a fixed price. RR. cen, queta.
2679: Pret. OCENQUIZ: in a game, to stake everything; to go out together, to gather; ti-cenquiça, we all go out. Tla — there is an abundance of everything necessary for life. Impers. cenquixoa, people gather. RR. cen, quica.
2680: With purity, perfectly. RR. cenquiçaliatli, ca.
2681: Gathering of persons, assembly, audience. R. cenquicça.
2682: adj. n. Twenty (blankets, goods, garments); lit. a bundle of clothes. RR. ce, quimilli.
2684: Gathering of persons, assembly, audience. R. cenquiça.
2685: Pret. OCENQUIXTI: nitla — to collect, to gather up objects that have been scattered. RR. cen, quixtia.
2686: (Olm.). Entirely, cleanly, perfectly; serves as a superlative: cenquizca chimi or zeni — to prime pahuacatzintli (Car.), completely pure; cenquizca qualli, perfect, extremely good, excellent; cenquizca mauiçotl, honor, supreme dignity. R. cenquizqui.
2687: Pret. OCENQUIZCAITTAC: nite — to succeed in fully knowing matters, to see nitla or perfectly a thing, to reason it through well. RR. cenquizca, itta.
2688: Pret. OCENQUIZCAQUALTILI: nite — to sanctify someone, to make them perfect, accomplished. RR. cenquizca, qualtilia.
2689: Whole, intact, clean, perfect. R. cenquiça.
2691: Pret. OCENQUIZTICATCA, v. aux. To be perfect, whole, intact, neither shared nor divided, speaking of an object or a place. RR. cenquiça, ca.
2692: Pret. OCENQUIZTOCA, v. n. To be quite near, full, entire; cenquiztoc in qualli in ilhuicac (Olm.), all is filled with good in heaven. RR. cenquiça, onoc.
2693: A stem of grass; lettuce, cabbage, etc.
2695: Pret. OCENTECAC: nitla or nic — to unite, arrange, coordinate, put in order, gather reasons and arguments in favor of something; to celebrate the exploits of someone by recounting or singing them. RR. cen, teca.
2696: n. A stem of a plant; lettuce, cabbage, etc. RR. ce, tecochtli.
2697: An only son or daughter. RR. centetl, conetl.
2842: pret. OGEQUALOC, v. n., pass. To freeze, to be frozen, speaking of a plant; in æochitl ocequaloc (Olm.), the flower was frozen; fig. he has fallen, declined from his greatness. RR. cel, qua.
2843: adj. v. Frozen, struck down, numb, dying of cold. R. cequulo.
2844: pret. to have a cramp. RR. cequi, uapaua, pitzraui.
2845: Some, something, a part of a whole; cequi opoliuito, something is missing there; centlamantin icheame cequi ichcatl, a part of a flock —
2846: — sheepskin; oc cequi, more, a little more. Pl. cequin or cequintin; oc cequintin, others. R. ce.
2847: n. used in comp. only: my close relative (brother or sister); that is to say: nocelca, one who is one with me, who comes from the same stock; icetca in ome atoyall, union, junction of two watercourses. RR. ce, ca.
2849: or CETIA, pret. OCETIAC or OCETIX: ni — to be one, to form a whole; ti-ceti or ti-cetia, to unite or do something together as a sign of friendship (Olm.); to join, to merge, to become one; cetia in atl, to come together, to flow together, speaking of several watercourses; cetia in toyollo, to be united in heart, to love one another; lit. our heart is but one; cetia in totlatol, to agree, to be of the same opinion; lit. our thought, our words are one, they accord; amo cetia totlatol, to disagree, to differ. R. ce.
2851: pret. OCETILI: nito — to do something together, to unite as a sign of friendship; mo-cetilia in nayotl, in tayotl, inic ocetia (for on-cetia) in eztli yolloli (Olm.), they have drawn close in their maternity and paternity, so as to be one by blood and heart. Nite — to unite, to marry the spouses; to reconcile, to make friends. Nitla — to unite, to join several things together, to form only one from them. R. cetia.
2852: Union, conformity of persons; unity. R. cetihia.
2853: Ice, black ice; cetl mo-pipiloa, icicle; lit. ice that rises.
2854: n. sister, etc.). In comp. nocetlacayo, my close relative. R. cetlacatl.
2856: theol. (The three divine persons) form a single whole (es uno el ser de las tres Close relative (brother or personas divinas ó es una su essencia); cuix tic-neltoca ca inic personas eittica, auh inic teotl çan cet-htica in totecuiyo Dios? (J.B.), do you believe that there are three persons in God and that he is alone in his essence?
2497: Finally, only once, all at once; cemi æ-iauh (Par.), go away then; and with more emphasis, çaz cemi: çaz cemi oniqu-ixnahuati, I sent him away completely. R. ce.
2498: Pret. OCEMIANILI: to hide everything someone has from them. RR. cemi, anilia.

2500: Lasting, eternal; cemicaca yoliliztli or yolihuayan (Gar.), place of eternal life. RR. cemicac, ca.
2501: Deep, sincere friendship. In comp. tecemicniuhyo, sincere friendship among people. R. cemicniuhth.
2502: Sincere friend, wholly affectionate. In comp. tecemicniuh, the friend of all. RR. cen, icniuhth.
2503: Pret. OCEMICXOTLAC: nite— to despise someone extremely, supremely. Nitla— to press, to tread upon a thing. RR. cen, icæotla.
2504: Pret. OCEMIHIYOU, etc.: nitla or nic— to suffer greatly, without respite, always, continually. RR. cen, thiyouia.
2505: Pret. OCEMILCAUH: nitla— to forget something entirely. RR. cen, ilcaua.
2506: One day's work of a farmer. RR. cemilhuitl, elimuctlhi.
2507: One day's journey on foot. RR. cemilhuitl, nenemihiztli.
2508: Task, work, one day's labor. RR. cemilhuitl, tequitl.
2509: Pret. OCEMILHUITI: ni— to remain a whole day somewhere, to tarry. R. cemilhuitl.
2510: During one day; cemilhuitica nican onino-tlali (Olm.), I stayed here one day. RR. cemilhuitl, ca.
2511: Food, nourishment, one day's ration. R. cemilhuitia.
2512: Pret. CEMILHUITILTI: nite— to keep someone for a day. R. cemilhuitia.
2513: One day, the space of a full day; aocmo uey cemilhuitl, a short day, not yet long; cemilhuitl cenyoal, day and night; cemilhuitl ipan nepantla tonatiuh, a day and a half; cemilhuitl mo-nenemi, one day's journey; cemilhuitl tlaxtlauilli, a day's wages; uel cemilhuitl or uel yuh cemilhuitl, all day, a full day. RR. ce, ilhuitl. —CEMILHUITLAELIMIC, adj. v. Ploughable in one day; quaquaue in cemilhuitlaelimic, extent of land that an ox can plough in one day. RR. cemilhuitl, elimiqui.
2514: n. Pay, wages for one day. RR. cemilhuitl, tlaxtlauilli.
2515: Pret. OCEMINAILII: nite— to hide someone's property. RR. cen, ina.
2516: n. Twenty (cloaks, mats, etc.); cemipill petlatl, twenty mats. When referring only to garments, one says: cenquimilli, onquimilli, etc. RR. ce, ipilli.
2958: Inhabitant of Chalco; pl. Chalca (Chim., Clav.). R. Chalco.
2959: Lord of Tepotzotlan, who was dispossessed by the king of Azcapugalco, Acolhuatzin.
2960: 5. (Clav.). Part of the coastal strip on the Gulf of Mexico, belonging to the province of Cuetlachtlan and where Vera-Cruz stands today.
2962: Or CHALCHIUITLICUE, n. (Clav.). Goddess of water, companion of the rain god, Tlaloc. She was also called Acuecueyotl, Ahuic, Ayauh, Apoçonallotl, Allacamani, Xixiquipilihui, etc.
2964: Brilliant, precious heart. In comp. ichalchiuhyollo, his excellent heart (Olm.). RR. chalchutl, yollotl.
2966: Pret. OCHALCHICHMA: nitla or nic — to take something with patience, to endure adversity, reprimands (Olm.). RR. chalchiuil, matr.

2969: With precious stones. RR. chalchiuitl, ca.
2970: Pret. OCHALCHIUHTILI: nite or nice — to obey, to be submissive; intla tic-chalchiuhtilizque (Olm.), if we obey him. R. chalchiuitl.
2971: First Toltec sovereign, who reigned at Tollan (Clav.).
2973: n. Raw emerald, pearl, green precious stone; fig. protector, young man, young girl; oc chalchiuitl, virgin, still a pearl. With the postposition teuh: chalchiuhteuh (Par.), like the emerald.

2976: n. Plant also called coxihuitl, black bryony, whose root is used as a remedy (Hern.). RR. ..... (?), cuitlatl.
2919: : dito — to dispute, to quarrel frequently, to rise up against one another. Mte or nitla — to frequently stir up people.
2921: Murmur, babble, chirping, noise of vessels being broken. R. chachalaca,
2924: : nitla — to strike one thing against another, to make a great deal of noise.
2925: Pret. OCHACHALOAC: ni — to speak loudly and angrily; to hiss, as geese do; to be broken, cracked, when speaking of an earthen vessel.
2926: Pret. OGHAGHALHUI: nitetla or nicte — to greatly increase the tribute, the tax; to damage, to destroy someone's property.
2927: Pret. OGHAGHALO, freq. of cHALoA: nitla — to make something hard.
2929: : nino — to boast, to highlight one's merits, to recount one's exploits. nite — to praise, to flatter, to adulate someone.
2931: Pret. OGHACHAPACAG, v. n. freq. of cuapani. To fall in large drops, to rain.
2932: Pret. OCHACHAPAN, v. n. cuapani. Same meaning.

2936: : nitla — to trample in the mud, to splash, to stain something.
2937: Pret. OCHAGHAQUACAC, v. n. freq. of cHAQUANI. To be splashed, to become dirty, speaking of an object.
2858: pret. ocencetzaqu: ni — to close completely; cen-cetzaqua (Olm.), to close well, completely. RR. cen, tzaqua.
2859: n. (Olm.). One who possesses cornstalks or ears of corn. R. centli.
2860: pret. oceuac, v. n. To be cold, to freeze. R. cetl.
2861: Rain; ceualatl mo-mana, to rain for a long time. RR. ceualh, atl.
2862: A hut covered with branches, gallery, belvedere. RR. ceualh, calli.

2864: A kind of wild vine (Hern.). RR. ceualli, chichiltic.

2867: Shady, dark, obscure; fig. protector (Olm.). R. ceualli.
2868: pret. to shade, to cover a thing with shadow. R. ceuallotl.
2869: Shady, having or casting shade. RR. ceuallotia, icac.
2870: Shade, shadow. With the postposition tlan: ceuallotitlan, in the shade, in the shadow. R. ceualli.
2871: Pret. oceuapauac: numbed, stiffened by the cold. RR. ceua, paua.
2873: Numbness caused by cold. R. ceuapaua.
2874: Pret. oceuatoya or oceuatoca, v. n.
2875: : nitla — to make; ni — to be seized, to be cold, to freeze. RR. ceua, onoc.
2876: Pret. oceuecapanoloc, Pass.: ni — to be raised exceedingly, forever. RR. cen, uecapanoa.
2998: lake on the western borders of the Mexican empire (Clav.).

3000: pret. OGHAPANI: nitla — to throw something to the ground, such as paste, mud, etc. R. chapani.

3007: (Olm.). Proud, cruel, speaking of a woman. RR. chapulin (?), ten.

2820: An entire age, an entire old age, a period of 104 years. RR. ce, ueuetiliztli.

2823: n. One spoonful. RR. ce, rumath.

2940: Pret. OCHAGHAQUAOHO: nitla — to make a thing rough.
2943: : nitla — to trample in the mud, to wet, to dirty a thing.

2950: Pret. OCHAYAUH, v. n. To spread, speaking of grain; to fall as snow; fig. ual chayaui in chalchiuul, in teoxiuitl (Olm.), the heart shows itself, reveals itself; lit. the emerald, the turquoise spread or shine.

2956: Pret. OCHALANITINEN — to seek quarrel; mo-chalanitinemi, he is quarrelsome, nino — one who likes to argue. RR. chalania, nemi.

2798: pret. OCENTOPEUH: mic — to disdain, to completely reject a thing. RR. cen, topeua.
2799: pret. OCENTZITZQUI: mic — to take a thing forever, to attach oneself to it, to persevere.
2801: 5. Centipede, a type of insect; lit. one who has four hundred hands.
2805: or CENTZUNTECOMATL, n. An ear of wheat or other grain. RR. ce, tzontecomatl.

2807: Bird that sings a great deal and imitates other birds; lit. one who has four hundred voices. RR. centzontli, tlatolli.

Four hundred; lit. a lock of hair, a handful of grass; centzontli ipan mallacpoalli, six hundred; lit. four hundred plus ten-twenties; centzontli ipan caxtolpoalli, seven hundred. With the postpositions can, pa: centzoncan, in four hundred places; centzompa, four hundred times. RR. ce, tzontli.

2813: pret. OCENUECAPANOLOC, pass.: ni — to be raised completely, always, perpetually. RR. cen, uecapanoa.

2816: adj. v. Almighty, all-powerful. RR. cen, uelitini.
2817: pret. OCENUETZ: together, at the same time. RR. cen, uetzi.
3020: (?) n. used in comp. only: no-chauh, my concubine,
3021: (Olm.). Proud, cruel, speaking of a woman.
3022: Pret. OCHAUHNECOGOYAG: M — to be possessed by the devil. RR. chauatl (?), cocoya.

Several species are known: chian pitzauac (Bét.), small chia; chantzotzolhi (Bét.), chia with white seeds that are toasted and then mixed with honey; etc. Also written cyan.

3029: or CHIELONI, adj. v. Worthy of being awaited; awaited. R. chia.

With the postposition co: techanco, in someone's house. Rev. ichantzinco in Dios (Olm.), in the house of God; ma æimo-calaqui mochantzinco, tlatoanie (Par.), enter, lord, into your house.
